errors.landingChat_intro: You are the expert sales assistant for REPUTEXA. You have access to a 'CURRENT DATA' block below: for any price, percentage, or offer detail, you must strictly adhere to it, do not invent figures.
errors.landingChat_keyboardCharter:
KEYBOARD STYLE (MANDATORY for all text you produce):
- No long dashes (em dash), no en dashes. No dashes with " - ": use commas, periods, or parentheses.
- Ellipses: three characters ... and never the single glyph "…".
- Straight quotes " and straight apostrophe ' if you use them.
- No non-breaking spaces; normal spaces.
errors.landingChat_groundedFactsCharter:
ACCURACY (MANDATORY):
- Do not invent any fact: no products, brands, sourcing locations, team names, hours, promotions, specific 'house' details, or figures or deadlines, unless explicitly stated in the provided context or system instructions.
- If information is missing, state it or remain deliberately general ('according to your feelings', 'as indicated on site') without fabricating.
- Never present a guess as a certainty.
errors.landingChat_roleAndProduct:
Role: provide precise information about the product, the Vision / Pulse / Zenith plans, the trial, multi-site billing, features (AI responses to reviews, alerts, PDFs, WhatsApp, SEO, Zenith review collection, etc.). Your tone is professional, reassuring, clear.

Product (summary):
- REPUTEXA = management of e-reputation for local SMEs (restaurants, accommodation, beauty, health, retail...).
- AI writes responses to reviews, with personalization (tone, length). Pulse/Zenith: WhatsApp alerts for very negative reviews, advanced reporting, shield/abuse reporting, ZENITH: Maps SEO layer, automated review collection, enhanced AI checks.
- 14-day trial; card to validate access; no charge during the trial period if the offer indicates it; cancellation from the client area.

Rules:
- Never promise 'guaranteed removal' of a review: talk about reporting procedures to platforms, proactive management, and compliance.
- If a question exceeds your knowledge base, state it honestly and refer to /pricing or the trial signup.
errors.landingChat_liveFacts:
=== CURRENT DATA (synchronized with config/pricing.ts, use these figures for any pricing question) ===
First establishment, monthly USD: Vision {visionUsd}, Pulse {pulseUsd}, Zenith {zenithUsd}.
First establishment, monthly EUR: Vision {visionEur}€, Pulse {pulseEur}€, Zenith {zenithEur}€.
First establishment, monthly GBP (UK site /en-gb): Vision {visionGbp}, Pulse {pulseGbp}, Zenith {zenithGbp}.
Add-ons (2nd, 3rd… establishment): tiered discounts via Stripe, approximately -20%, -30%, -40%, then -50% on subsequent ones.
Annual billing: -20% on total (display type 'equivalent / month' on the site).
Trial: 14 days on plans; card to validate access; simple cancellation from the client area.
Pages: /pricing (offer details), /signup?mode=trial (trial).

PricingPage.headline: Choose Your Plan
PricingPage.subtitle: Free Trial · No Commitment
PricingPage.paymentCancelledToast: Payment not completed. Your options have been saved.
PricingPage.resumeCheckoutToast: You had started a subscription. Would you like to continue?
PricingPage.stripeReassurance: Secure payment via Stripe. No commitment.
PricingPage.trialMention: [[PX:zero]] today. Card required for access validation. Cancel with one click from your customer area.
PricingPage.prorataMessage: Prorated charges are calculated automatically. You only pay the difference at the time of the change.
PricingPage.zenithDataConsent: I confirm that my customers are informed of the use of their contact details for soliciting reviews (WhatsApp), in accordance with GDPR.
PricingPage.legalCheckHint: Check this box to enable payment.
PricingPage.planBlockedZenith: Accept the terms and customer confirmation to continue.
PricingPage.visionDesc: Gateway: save time and maintain a clean Google profile, without the toolkit of higher plans.
PricingPage.visionFeature1: Automated, truly personalized review responses: AI reads every sentence and responds to the customer's words (e.g., 'thank you for the complimentary coffee' → the response mentions this gesture). Strict formality, echoing customer phrasing, professional finish.
PricingPage.visionFeature2: Monthly PDF reporting via email: a health report of a few pages — volume, average rating, trends. Factual observations, not the 'consulting firm' level of higher plans.
PricingPage.visionFeature3: Mastered human tone: no one should think it's a machine — polished syntax, just the right amount of emotion, never excessive.
PricingPage.visionFeature4: One language per account: that of your market (e.g., Spanish company → responses in natural Spanish). Negative reviews do not go out via WhatsApp: they arrive in the Alerts tab of the dashboard with modifiable AI suggestions, then a publication queue (human delay), like other responses.
PricingPage.visionFeature5: Vision scope: no real-time WhatsApp alerts, no automated customer collection, no advanced SEO boost or triple engine — switch to Pulse or Zenith for these features.
PricingPage.pulseDesc: Peace of mind: active monitoring, challenging reviews on WhatsApp, reports that tell you what to do.
PricingPage.pulseFeature1: Same writing standards as Vision: line-by-line analysis, customer words echoed, formality, no generic messages.
PricingPage.pulseFeature2: 1–2★ review alerts on WhatsApp (~30s): you see the review, the proposed response; modify by text or voice, then Publish → enters the queue with a realistic publication timer.
PricingPage.pulseFeature3: Monthly 'consultant' PDF: beyond the numbers — comparison with past months, recurring themes, concrete action points like a strategist (predictive sentiment/internal audit when data allows).
PricingPage.pulseFeature4: Weekly WhatsApp recap (Monday 8 AM UTC): clear figures, what’s going well / what’s not, direct tone like a consultant — plus detailed weekly history in Statistics.
PricingPage.pulseFeature5: Toxic review shield: alert in the dashboard + structured reporting file (AI) to review and submit; threatening/offensive reviews may also trigger an immediate WhatsApp with a link to Alerts.
PricingPage.pulseFeature6: AI indistinguishable from a human in form: not too 'robotically polite', just right.
PricingPage.pulseFeature7: Triple internal draft + selection of the best response (social psychology criteria) before sending to the queue.
PricingPage.pulseFeature8: Native multilingual responses (not Google Translate) for tourists and international clientele.
PricingPage.zenithDesc: Marketing director 24/7: all Pulse features, plus SEO Maps, review capture, and triple AI safeguards.
PricingPage.zenithFeature1: Review responses: same quality promise — fine reading, customer echo, formality, anti-repetition control.
PricingPage.zenithFeature2: Triple AI Verification (three different logics + judge): the most convincing response goes to the queue.
PricingPage.zenithFeature3: Negative review alerts on WhatsApp + editable suggestions + publication queue, just like Pulse.
PricingPage.zenithFeature4: Enhanced tactical/strategic monthly PDF + link with your weekly recaps for a vision that matures over time.
PricingPage.zenithFeature5: Weekly WhatsApp recap + archives by week in Statistics (property filter).
PricingPage.zenithFeature6: SEO Maps boost: weak keywords detected, naturally injected into responses and WhatsApp solicitations (capture).
PricingPage.zenithFeature7: Same toxic review shield as Pulse: prepared file in Alerts + immediate WhatsApp for serious threats.
PricingPage.zenithFeature8: Automated review collection via WhatsApp (checkout/webhook), clear consent, human tone — as already planned in REPUTEXA.
PricingPage.zenithFeature10: Native multilingual on all customer-facing components.
PricingPage.visionDetailsExtended:
What you get concretely with Vision:

• Responses generated by premium AI models (like GPT/Claude depending on our stack), with anti-'robot' guidelines: variable structure from one response to another, echoing the customer's exact phrasing, formality, zero generic copy-pasting.

• Reviews of 3 stars and above generally follow the automatic flow: the response is prepared immediately, then published after a queue delay of a few hours (the 'human response time' effect), with a visible countdown. As long as it is in the queue, you can still modify the text.

• More severe reviews (less than 3 stars) do not go out via WhatsApp: they arrive in the Alerts tab of the dashboard, with an AI suggestion that you validate or rewrite before publication queueing — as planned for this plan.

• Monthly PDF via email: factual health report (volume, average rating, trend) on a few pages. This is not the same 'consulting firm/predictive' level as on Pulse or Zenith.

• One primary language aligned with your market. Multi-property: each site has its own views (reviews, stats, alerts); when managing multiple, some sends (e.g., reports) can also be thought of globally.

What is not included in Vision: real-time WhatsApp alerts on negative reviews, automated customer collection via WhatsApp, advanced SEO boost on Maps, triple cross-checking between multiple AI logics — reserved for Pulse and Zenith plans. After subscription, everything related to tone, length of responses, and your personal guidelines remains adjustable in Settings.
PricingPage.pulseDetailsExtended:
Pulse retains the Vision promise and adds active monitoring:

• Same writing requirement (line-by-line reading, customer words echoed, credible human tone). On the engine side: multiple drafts are compared internally and only the most convincing response goes to your publication queue.

• Reviews of 1 or 2 stars trigger a WhatsApp alert in very little time: you see the review, the proposed response, you can modify it (text or voice), then validate the publication — which goes back through the queue with a realistic delay, just like other reviews.

• Monthly 'consultant' PDF: beyond the raw numbers — comparison with previous months, recurring themes, action points, and audit-type readings or trends when data supports analysis.

• Every Monday at 8 AM (UTC): WhatsApp recap (direct tone, figures, what’s going well / what’s blocking) and, in the same movement, a more complete version archived in Statistics, navigable week by week.

• Hate reviews or those clearly against Google rules: structured file prepared in Alerts for review and reporting; serious cases may also trigger an immediate WhatsApp with a link to the Alerts page.

• Responses in multiple languages, treated as native (not a simple automatic translation). Multi-property: selector at the top of the dashboard, data kept separate by site for the most part; recaps can emphasize the most critical property while maintaining an overall view.
PricingPage.zenithDetailsExtended:
Zenith includes everything in Pulse, with the 'marketing & SEO' layer:

• Review responses: same quality charter, plus a triple verification protocol (multiple AI 'voices' that will challenge the selected response) to minimize errors or awkwardness before sending to the queue.

• Google Maps SEO boost: identification of intentions/keywords where you are weak, then natural integration into responses and WhatsApp review collection messages — without visible advertising blocks.

• Automated review collection via WhatsApp after payment (checkout, webhook, etc.), with explicit consent and human tone — as already planned in the REPUTEXA ecosystem.

• Negative review alerts on WhatsApp, editable suggestions, toxic files in Alerts: same principles as Pulse, with an additional layer of tools for multi-site groups.

• Monthly PDF reports and weekly recaps designed to link short-term (weekly action) and medium-term (monthly priorities). Multiple properties: internal rankings, comparisons, and deliverables tailored in the spirit of 'holding'.

• Publication queue, timers, and the ability to edit until it’s sent: identical to other plans, to maintain a consistent image on the customer side. Tone, length, and personalized AI guidelines can be adjusted in Settings; each location can be refined over time.
PricingPage.compareSubtitle: Vision, Pulse, and Zenith: same definitions as on the pricing page, presented side by side for a comprehensive view.
PricingPage.compareModalHint: ✓ included in the plan · ✗ not included in this plan. Contractual detail: Terms of Service and Pricing page.
PricingPage.tooltipCompareAll: Opens a detailed matrix: Vision, Pulse, and Zenith features side by side.
PricingPage.tooltipBillingCycle: Switch between paying every month and paying once per year. The annual badge shows the savings.
PricingPage.tooltipBadgePulse: Most often chosen: real-time alerts, weekly recaps, and advanced AI response quality.
PricingPage.tooltipBadgeZenith: Full stack: Pulse features plus SEO boost, capture, and triple AI verification.
PricingPage.compareMatrixFootnote: ✓ / ✗ = included or not in the product offering. For the definitive legal framework (Zenith, end customers, durations), see the Terms of Service and privacy policy.
PricingPage.matrixRow_aiPersonalized_detail: Each text starts from the actual content of the review — no stock phrases. Goal: a professional, credible, and human tone, with anti-repetition guidelines to avoid the 'robot' effect.
PricingPage.matrixRow_humanQueue_detail: The response does not go out immediately: a realistic delay enhances credibility. Until the moment of publication, you can still modify the text in the dashboard.
PricingPage.matrixRow_singleLanguage: Vision: One Primary Language for the Entire Account
PricingPage.matrixRow_singleLanguage_detail: Adapted for a stable clientele in one language (e.g., establishment in the US → responses in English). This is not the advanced 'native' multilingual feature of Pulse and Zenith plans.
PricingPage.matrixRow_multilingual_detail: The review is detected in its language; the response is written in that same language, naturally — useful for tourists, cross-border clients, or reviews in English/Spanish/etc.
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleDraft: Multiple Internal Drafts, One Selected Response
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleDraft_detail: AI generates and compares several versions; only the strongest (clarity, empathy, coherence with the review) is proposed to you and then queued, to limit weak responses.
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleJudge: Zenith: Triple Verification Before Sending to Queue
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleJudge_detail: Three different approaches analyze the response, then a 'judge' layer decides: the goal is to reduce awkwardness, inaccuracies, or risky formulations before publication.
PricingPage.matrixRow_pdfBasic_detail: Volume of reviews, average rating, trend: the essentials to track your e-reputation without PowerPoint slide jargon.
PricingPage.matrixRow_pdfConsultant_detail: Beyond the numbers: recurring themes, inter-month comparisons, concrete action points; sometimes 'audit' or trend readings when data allows.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappBad: Very Negative Reviews (1–2★): WhatsApp Alert + Draft
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappBad_detail: You quickly receive the review and a proposed response on WhatsApp, editable before publication. In Vision, these reviews go into Alerts without real-time WhatsApp alerts.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappWeekly: Weekly WhatsApp Recap + History in Statistics
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappWeekly_detail: Every week: summary of figures and key topics; a detailed version is archived to retrieve the history week by week.
PricingPage.matrixRow_toxicShield: Toxic or Threatening Reviews: File & Reporting Assistance
PricingPage.matrixRow_toxicShield_detail: Structured file in the dashboard to prepare a request to Google with arguments to review; serious cases may also trigger an immediate WhatsApp alert.
PricingPage.matrixRow_mapsSeo: Zenith: SEO Boost for Google Maps
PricingPage.matrixRow_mapsSeo_detail: Identifies intentions or phrases where you are weak and suggests integrating them naturally into your public responses and, if necessary, in solicitation messages — without stuffing or aggressive advertising tone.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappCapture: Zenith: Review Collection via WhatsApp After Purchase
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappCapture_detail: After payment (checkout, e-commerce webhook, etc.), a thread can invite feedback or reviews based on your settings and displayed legal mentions. REPUTEXA is the technical tool; you remain responsible for informing your end customers (GDPR).
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappSampling_detail: WhatsApp review invitations are not sent to every eligible customer: the platform uses controlled sampling (by default about one in ten eligible checkouts, configurable) to cap volume, avoid a spammy perception, and keep the channel credible. Public Google replies echo the review wording per dashboard tone and length settings.
PricingPage.matrixRow_cooldown120_detail: Same number, same merchant account: no new solicitation campaign before 120 days after the start of the previous one — the platform blocks below this. After 120 days, a new solicitation becomes possible if the number is not opposed (STOP). Identifiers in the queue are anonymized or deleted no later than 120 days (see privacy policy).
PricingPage.legalAcceptCheckbox: I accept the <terms>Terms of Service</terms> and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy> of REPUTEXA.

variants.review: Adorable welcome, buttery croissants just like in the countryside. The birthday order with a name on the brioche: a flawless execution. My mother said it was her best galette in ten years.
variants.response: Thank you Camille for this feedback that makes the whole team shine. We are delighted that the galette and the birthday order have fulfilled you: this is exactly the artisanal level we strive to maintain daily.

variants.review: Breathtaking view at breakfast, firm bedding just how we like it, and the local gift box in the room was a rare touch. The staff took the time to explain the hikes — we recommend it to slow travel lovers.
variants.response: Thank you so much Thomas and Léa: reading that the bedding and panoramic breakfast enhanced your stay motivates us daily. We are also pleased that the hiking tips pleased you; we look forward to welcoming you for another season by the lake.
variants.seoContext: hotel lake view comfort slow travel

variants.review: Complete service + tyres before winter: everything was shown to me, clear invoice without surprises. They spotted a disc play before I broke down on the A7. Professional and human service, rare at a garage.
variants.response: Thank you Karim for this trust: showing the parts and anticipating the disc play is part of our 'zero bad surprise' philosophy. See you soon for the next service or a quick check.

variants.review: Completely botched cut when I showed three photos + video; the shade has nothing to do with it. I was charged for a keratin treatment without being informed of the price. I leave humiliated and angry.

problem.headline: The Silent Threat: Unmanaged Customer Feedback.
problem.intro: Every day, restaurateurs lose hours managing reviews, suffer devastating 1-star ratings, and fail to amplify positive feedback. One bad review can destroy your Google rating—and your bookings.
problem.solution: Introducing your 24/7 Customer Relations Director. An AI that never sleeps, protects your reputation, and transforms satisfied customers into ambassadors.

negativeFilter.headline: The Ultimate Shield: How We Eradicate 1-Star Reviews.
negativeFilter.step1: The AI contacts the customer via WhatsApp: 'How was your experience?'
negativeFilter.step2: If negative, the AI responds: 'We are sorry. Rather than publishing on Google, would you prefer to contact the owner privately to resolve this?'
negativeFilter.step3: The customer expresses privately. The owner receives a discreet WhatsApp alert.
negativeFilter.result: The 1-star review never appears on Google. This feature alone is worth more than the subscription.

tunnel.title: The REPUTEXA conversation flow
tunnel.subtitle: Goal: improve service with honest feedback — not a blunt review ask. When the customer confirms, AI prepares paste-ready text in the clipboard; one button opens the Google or TripAdvisor listing: they paste, pick their star rating, and publish — no retyping, and they always have the final say.

1.title: First touch (the right moment)
1.body: After purchase, on the delay you choose, a short warm message asks for two minutes to share what worked or what could be better — yes or no, clearly. If they decline or send STOP: a simple thank-you, no more follow-ups on this topic, and they are blocklisted for this flow (duration per your settings, often 120 days or permanent).

2.title: Receipt context → human question
2.body: If they say yes, the chat uses what we know from the visit — ticket, service, package — to invite a few words on what landed or what felt off: conversational tone, not a survey.

3.title: Paste-ready review text
3.body: Once feedback is in, AI suggests a short line that keeps their wording and a believable local touch — already in the clipboard. They can still edit however they want (typing, voice, photos) until they are happy; every tweak is taken into account.

4.title: Button → review page, paste, stars
4.body: After they confirm the draft, the business does not send one long message: first a short reminder to copy the draft (long-press the message above), then a separate message with only the Google write-review link. In production, a smart link can also copy the text automatically and then open Google (intermediate page). They paste, pick their star rating, and publish.

5.title: A sincere thank-you
5.body: A final clear message: thanks for their time, reminder that contact details will not be reused for this outreach, and that data tied to this request is processed until deletion within the window shown at the point of sale — often within 120 days in practice.

The Google preview shows a public reply that echoes the reviewer’s words, matching dashboard tone, length, and instructions like in real use.

testimonials.headline: What professionals around the world are saying
testimonials.subtitle: +2,400 establishments manage their reputation with REPUTEXA.
testimonials.highlightCountLabel: {count, plural, one {# client testimonial} other {# client testimonials}}

sophie.quote: The intelligent filter alone has saved us. We had 2-3 unjust reviews per month that were dragging us down. Now? Zero public catastrophes. It's worth every penny for peace of mind.
sophie.name: Sophie M.

marco.quote: It's like having a reputation director 24/7. I receive WhatsApp alerts for emergencies, and the AI manages the rest. Our rating went from 3.9 to 4.8 in 4 months. Unmissable.
marco.name: Marco D.

isabelle.quote: Serenity is what I pay for. The ZENITH plan is the best investment we've made: review collection and AI responses run effortlessly and our Google visibility has exploded.
isabelle.name: Isabelle R.

matteo.quote: Finally an AI that understands the rhythm of an Italian restaurant: reviews come in during service, and I no longer have to choose between the dining room and Google. In a few weeks the rating rises and the comments better describe what really pleases. Even on a busy Saturday, the responses remain worthy of the house. I can finally sleep without monitoring the stars at 2 am.
matteo.name: Matteo G.

yuki.quote: In Kyoto, we welcome travellers from all over the world; on Google, expectations vary according to cultures. WhatsApp alerts signal the urgent, and responses respect our codes of kindness without sounding cold. Our clients see the same requirement online as in the ryokan. It has become a team reflex, not an administrative chore.
yuki.name: Yuki T.

james.quote: Pub in London: the Maps rating went from 3.8 to 4.7, and recent reviews finally tell the story of our cuisine, not just the area. Regulars return and tourists find us without passing by. The AI keeps an authentic pub tone, not a corporate speech. Frankly, we would have taken quarters to achieve this consistency manually.
james.name: James W.

lukas.quote: In Bavaria, word of mouth remains king, but before a reservation, people read Google like a second opinion. REPUTEXA saves us hours each week while maintaining a warm house tone. Responses evoke our seasonal dishes and our actual hours without errors. Today the listing reflects the welcome we give in the dining room.
lukas.name: Lukas M.

elena.quote: Busy season in Andalusia: while I coordinate service in the kitchen, the AI responds to each review with the detail I wouldn't have time to write between services. Travellers see that we truly read what they liked or regretted. Our rating has stabilised without sounding artificial. Essential when the terrace is fully booked four nights a week.
elena.name: Elena R.

thiago.quote: In Rio, Google reviews weigh as much as a friend's recommendation even before the client calls. Our churrascaria has doubled bookings in three months because every recent feedback receives a quick and warm response. The tool keeps our Brazilian voice, not a generic model. The team in the dining room focuses on service while the online reputation follows.
thiago.name: Thiago S.

chen.quote: For our hotel in Shanghai, international travellers compare several listings long before booking. Quick, consistent responses in the right tone have transformed how we are perceived outside of China. We can address remarks in English or Chinese without unrealistic delays. It has become a conversion lever as important as the photos.
chen.name: Chen Wei

sarah.quote: Quick service in Texas: our customers expect warmth and spontaneity, not corporate boilerplate. AI responses maintain the tone of our diner, even when Sunday brunch is full. Every review receives a response that shows we really read it. Today I no longer juggle between the grill and Google alerts on my phone.
sarah.name: Sarah J.

googleMaps.q: Can the AI really help me rank higher on Google?
googleMaps.a: Yes. Google (the search engine and maps) favours establishments that respond quickly and accurately. Our AI ensures 24/7 responsiveness. For the ZENITH plan, it strategically injects SEO keywords into your responses to signal to the algorithm that you are the reference in your sector. The more you respond intelligently, the more Google promotes you over your competitors.

iaDetectable.q: Will my customers realise it's AI?
iaDetectable.a: Absolutely not. Our models are trained on thousands of real merchant responses. The tone is warm, professional, and indistinguishable from a human response. For the ZENITH plan, the 'Triple Verification' compares three response variants and only publishes the best. Your customers will feel listened to by a human, with the added time savings.

installTime.q: How long does it take to install?
installTime.a: Less than 2 minutes. You connect your Google Business account, and the AI starts analysing your reviews immediately. There is no software to install; everything happens on your REPUTEXA dashboard or directly on WhatsApp for alerts.

gratuit14j.q: Is it really free for 14 days?
gratuit14j.a: Yes, completely. You enjoy the power of REPUTEXA without spending a penny for two weeks. Although we use Stripe to secure access, no amount is charged before the end of your trial period. You can cancel with one click if you are not impressed by the results. It’s an opportunity to test the impact on your reputation with no risk.

adminComplianceKit.disclaimer: This page provides links only. It does not replace legal advice or DPAs with suppliers.
adminComplianceKit.sectionOperatorChecklist: Operator sheet — all admin (without forgetting anything)
adminComplianceKit.operatorChecklistIntro: Agents and automations do a lot: this sheet is what only a human validates, decides, or signs. Check off as you go; each action is logged. Use 'Archive my trace' to freeze a state (proof of diligence). Resets erase the day/week/month boxes, not the history.
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_security_badge: Security & Perfection: score, activity log, no alert left unattended
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_ghost_shield: Ghost Shield / emergency stop: desired state for production, documented if active
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_nexus_slack: Nexus · Support: every opened file is read, decided, or routed (nothing 'sleeps')
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_security_audit: Security: review last audit summary and fixes (paper or backlog)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_blackbox_sample: Black Box: test search + restore on a recent real extract
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_ia_queue: IA Forge: RLHF queue / human feedback — sorted according to your policy
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_guardian_stats: Code Guardian: debt, last export, trend — you prioritize the follow-ups
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_dpia_review: DPIA / transfers / registers: monthly review with compliance referent
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_subprocessors_csv: Subcontractors: CSV / updated list, signed DPAs and noted dates
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_investor_archive: Investors: metrics / report — close or archive the period
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_legal_pages: Public legal pages: aligned with the reality of suppliers & processing
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_full_export: Sensitive audit exports — deposited in private space, never in Git
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_drill_emergency: Emergency stop drill: who calls whom, where is the written procedure
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_access_review: Access: review admin accounts, keys, Slack, hosts — least privilege
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_m_legal_pages: → Legal pages section of this file
adminComplianceKit.operatorTraceIntro: Timestamped history (checks, unchecks, resets, archives). Kept in base with the file.
adminComplianceKit.operatorArchiveNotePlaceholder: Optional note (e.g.: end of sprint, before CNIL audit, after incident…)
adminComplianceKit.operatorArchiveHelp: Records the checked boxes' state + your note. Useful for proving regular human review.
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_hub_pulse: Admin home: browse modules, stats, and absence of red alerts (2–5 min)
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_d_hub_pulse: Overview — you, not just the automations.
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_clients_fire: Clients: list queue — priority 100, security alerts and exceptions seen today
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_legal_guardian_inbox: Legal: Guardian drafts / publications — nothing blocking without your decision
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_sentinel_compliance: Sentinel / production health: 360 flash or maintenance panel reviewed
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_council_digest: Digest 'agent council': read the last cron summary (understanding, not execution)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_vault_sentinel: Sentinel vault / backups: last run OK, escalate if failure
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_billing_stripe: Billing: human review of overdue, churn, volumes (Stripe + base consistency)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_external_audit_walk: External auditor walk-through 20–30 min: read-only navigation, no intervention
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_o_external_audit_walk: → This file + admin home as guest if possible
adminComplianceKit.sectionDocHabitsTitle: Document habits guide — files & proofs (outside admin boxes)
adminComplianceKit.sectionDocHabitsIntro: These boxes help you not forget around public documents and Drive. They stay on this device (browser); the operator sheet below is saved in base.
adminComplianceKit.habitDocPeriodWeekly: Every week (30–45 min total, spread out)
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_d_readme: Open the README of the kit 5 minutes
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_d_readme: Know where the checklist, exports, sensitive locations are.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_d_legal_glance: Test the 3 legal pages of the site
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_d_legal_glance: Privacy, terms, mentions — blank page or error = to be corrected immediately.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_w_guide: Browse a section of the complete verification guide
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_w_guide: Not all at once: one section per week (frequencies, limits of perfect percentage, inventory).
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_w_fortresse: GDPR, work, B2B, tech — one pillar per week to keep the vision.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_csv: Update the subcontractors list on Drive + DPA dates
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_csv: The CSV template from the kit is the base; the legal truth lives outside Git.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_registre: Update the Article 30 register (CSV or HTML→PDF) and archive the dated version
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_registre: One line or section per real processing; countries, purposes, subcontractors aligned.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_export_sample: Make an audit export (JSON or CSV) and store it in the right private place
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_export_sample: Even for trying: check that you know where the file should live and who accesses it.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_law: Open legal question? It's tracked with the counsel or DPO
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_law: The app organizes; it does not decide for you.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_dpa: Every critical subcontractor has an up-to-date DPA in the internal file
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_dpa: Link to the public policy does not replace a signed contract.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_human_trace: At least one 'human' archive this month (operator sheet or export)
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_human_trace: Simple proof that someone flesh and blood supervised.
adminComplianceKit.habitDocLocalNote: Local storage: another computer or cleared cache = boxes reset.
adminComplianceKit.sectionBeyondAppIntro: The compliance kit page is a framework. Maturity comes from the real work behind.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletLaw: Legal advice and positioning (DPIA, transfers, countries, clauses): competent human.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletDpa: Content of private files (Drive, signed DPAs, proofs outside code).
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletDrive: Regularity: without the habit of checking, archiving, and exporting, the tool remains a beautiful shell.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletHabit: Agents do a lot; the final signature of risks remains yours.
adminComplianceKit.kitFilesCadenceIntro: Each line indicates the recommended frequency. These are not legal obligations: they are guidelines to not forget anything. The detail (§7–§12) is in the complete guide.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmTitle: Summary — the whole page in rhythm
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmDaily: Every day: 'habits guide' boxes (browser) + operator sheet 'day'; open the 3 legal pages; 5 min on the kit README.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmWeekly: Every week: one section of the complete guide + one Fortress pillar; operator sheet 'week'.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmMonthly: Every month: subcontractors CSV (Drive), HTML register→PDF dated, audit export stored outside Git, 'human' archive (sheet or export); operator sheet 'month'; compliance file PDF archived automatically on the 1st (previous month, UTC) or manually from the kit.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmEvent: At each event: new provider, legal publication, PII migration, incident — open the complete guide §7.1 and follow the mini-checklist.
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqReadme: Daily (~5 min) · also before audit or after long break
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqGuide: No reading in one block: 1 section/week OR at each big change OR checklist §8 before control
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqFortresse: Weekly: 1 pillar in rotation (4 weeks = complete cycle)
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqCsv: At each new subcontractor / DPA · monthly list review · quarterly DPA links
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqRegistreHtml: At each modified processing · dated PDF archived (Drive) at least in monthly review / before audit
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqRegistreCsv: Identical to HTML (same info, spreadsheet format for DPO)
adminComplianceKit.sectionLegalCadence: Daily: test the 3 URLs (error = immediate correction). After legal publication admin: recheck display.
adminComplianceKit.sectionAuditExportsCadence: Monthly: a test export + documented private location. Before control or merchant file: targeted exports.
adminComplianceKit.sectionSensitiveCadence: Once at startup, then at each tool or logs URL change; up-to-date copy on Drive (do not commit sensitive URLs).
adminComplianceKit.auditJsonCadence: Same rhythm as the section (monthly / before control / merchant file). 120-day window. Single file: review_queue + consent_logs — to be stored outside Git.
adminComplianceKit.auditCsvQueueCadence: Same calendar. 'Queue' table in Excel; message text column only if the sensitive box is checked.
adminComplianceKit.auditCsvConsentCadence: Same calendar. WhatsApp consent logs (consent/refusal/STOP, hash) — separate file, without message bodies.
adminComplianceKit.bundlePdfIntro: A single PDF: subcontractors, emplacements-logs template, register (text derived from HTML), journal and frozen archives of the operator sheet for the chosen month (UTC), state of the boxes at generation, signature. Download without archiving, or 'Archive on vault' for the same file in Storage. The cron on the 1st of the month automatically archives the previous civil month (UTC). Store on Drive if you take out a copy — not in Git.
adminComplianceKit.bundlePdfHint: Recommended rhythm: 1st of the month or after the monthly review. The 'perfect' version of the register remains the print from the browser on the HTML URL if an auditor requires the original layout.
adminComplianceKit.bundleArchivesEmpty: No archive yet — after the 1st cron of the month or a manual archiving, files will appear here.
adminComplianceKit.archivesLoadError: Unable to load the archive list.
adminComplianceKit.bundleCronAutoHint: Every 1st of the month (Vercel cron, same secret as the others), the previous UTC month is generated and deposited here if an auto archive does not already exist for this period.
adminComplianceKit.bundleArchiveDone: Archived on admin vault — list updated.

defiReputexa.title: REPUTEXA Challenge
defiReputexa.introObjective: Over a period you choose, the team motivates to have clients leave reviews filled with compliments — ideally mentioning a name when someone made a difference. Everyone sees their impact on the ranking; you set the rewards to encourage full participation.
defiReputexa.introMechanics: In practice: a window of 1 week to 1 month (often 1 to 2 times a year), a bonus paragraph in WhatsApp during the challenge, points only for the names you track, a live individual ranking. The prizes and tiers: you write them yourself; REPUTEXA calculates the points and displays the leaderboard.
defiReputexa.rulesSubtitle: A review only counts if a name from your list appears in the comment (or resembles the displayed name of the reviewer — approximation, no legal value). Otherwise: 0 points.
defiReputexa.rule5Text: Tracked name spotted in the review → three points for that person.
defiReputexa.rule4Text: Same logic: one point for the mentioned person.
defiReputexa.ruleLowText: Tracked name spotted in a very negative review → minus one point for that person (if no tracked name matches, nothing happens).
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionMessage: Text added to WhatsApp messages (active period)
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionPlaceholder: E.g.: A little internal animation — if someone particularly impressed you, you can mention their name…
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionHint: This block is added after the usual message as long as the box is checked, the dates are filled, and we are within the window.
defiReputexa.fieldRewardsPlaceholder: E.g.: 1st: gift voucher $100 · 2nd: team meal · 3rd: half-day off…
defiReputexa.fieldNamesHint: The score looks for these strings in the review text and the displayed name of the reviewer (approximation — no legal proof).
defiReputexa.leaderboardSubtitle: Reviews imported between the challenge dates. Only the names you recorded below are taken into account.
defiReputexa.totalPointsHint: Sum of all personal points over the period.
defiReputexa.leaderboardEmpty: No tracked employee for this scoring (add names and save).
defiReputexa.rewardsAttributionTitle: Final ranking and reminder of rewards
defiReputexa.rewardsAttributionIntro: Once the end date has passed, the table below reminds everyone’s position and the points obtained. The block 'your rewards' repeats verbatim what you wrote: you decide alone on their implementation with the team.
defiReputexa.rewardsMissingNote: You did not provide any rewards text — remember to keep track internally of what each place receives.
defiReputexa.rewardsDisclaimer: The actual allocation of prizes is solely the responsibility of your company. REPUTEXA displays the ranking and your text as a reminder.
defiReputexa.employeeReviewsSubtitle: Highlighted excerpts of the tracked first name or last name spotted in the comment (approximation, no legal value).
defiReputexa.employeeReviewsEmpty: No reviews yet counting points for this person during the challenge period.
defiReputexa.emptyTracked: Add and save the tracked names to display the ranking and details by review.
defiReputexa.liveBoardHint: Updated when a new review is imported. Flash cards green or pink during a points change.
defiReputexa.archiveHint: When the end date has passed, archive to keep the ranking below and reset the form for the next challenge.
defiReputexa.archiveOk: Challenge archived. You can launch a new one.
defiReputexa.archiveError: Archiving failed (check that the end date has passed).
defiReputexa.archivesSubtitle: From most recent to oldest — same scoring and summary saved at the time of archiving.
defiReputexa.topHeroIntro: Overview of the tunnel your clients receive: the idea is primarily to improve service through their feedback — not to demand a review upfront. When the challenge period is active (box checked + dates), the block you write below also adds to the sends and free expression — soft tone, no insistence.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep1: Step 1 — After the chosen delay: message focused on service improvement; explicit yes/no request to grant ~2 minutes. STOP at any time.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep2: Step 2 — The client shares their experience (text or voice). If the challenge is active: optional paragraph about the ongoing animation and the possibility to mention a name, only if they wish.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep3: Step 3 — The AI can propose a natural text using **their words** (discreet SEO). The client has the final say: unlimited edits via keyboard, voice, or photos until satisfaction.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep4: Step 4 — Copy to clipboard + link to publish on Google in two clicks.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep5: Step 5 — Thank you and reminder of compliance (as on your poster / your commitments).
defiReputexa.topHeroStop: STOP: immediate stop with a polite closure message.
defiReputexa.teamLinkIntro: Without connection to the dashboard: paste this link in your group for everyone to see the updated ranking.
defiReputexa.teamLinkScope: Display: reviews from the main profile during the challenge period (secondary properties: coming soon).
defiReputexa.teamLinkPending: Save the challenge once to activate the permanent link.
defiReputexa.fieldActiveHint: The box remains checked after Save. The bonus 'challenge' messages only go out during the date window; at the end, everything automatically returns to the standard journey until the next challenge.
defiReputexa.scheduledHint: The dates have not yet started. From the first day, automatic sends will include the challenge block (if you wrote it).
defiReputexa.endedBannerBody: The ranking below is frozen on these dates. Archive whenever you want to freeze the recap and prepare for the next challenge, or uncheck 'Activate' to cut the challenge mode without archiving.
defiReputexa.journeyCompareTitle: What does the WhatsApp journey look like?
defiReputexa.journeyCompareSubtitle: On the left: normal flow. On the right: what gets added when the challenge is active and we are within your dates (soft tone, never insistent).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1Delay: About the delay you choose after the purchase (time to settle in or finish the meal).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1MsgStandard: ‘Hello [First Name]! This is the team from [Establishment]. Our goal is mainly to improve: can we count on two little minutes from you to tell us what you liked or what we could do better after your visit? Reply yes or no as it’s easiest for you.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep1MsgChallenge: Same opening message; the reminder ‘little team competition’ comes in the next step, only if the client said yes.
defiReputexa.journeyStep1BranchNo: If no: polite closure message, no further follow-up for this device, and the contact is blacklisted for this collection (no recontact for this program, according to your settings).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1Stop: At any time: STOP → same closure, blacklist for this collection, immediate stop.
defiReputexa.journeyStep2Context: The AI knows the context (e.g. lunch formula + coffee). The client responds in text or voice.
defiReputexa.journeyStep2MsgStandard: ‘Great, thanks! In a few words, how was your [product/service]? What did you like, and is there a detail we could do better for you?’
defiReputexa.journeyStep2MsgChallenge: ‘Great, thanks! In a few words, how was your [product/service]? Did a team member make your moment special? This month we’re playing a little game internally to aim for excellence 🏆: if you wish, you can mention a name and say what you liked, or what could have gone better.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep2Footer: Your personalized text (the 'Challenge' field) can complement this tone; it remains optional and light.
defiReputexa.journeyStep3Example: The client writes e.g.: ‘It was great, very good coffee and the blonde waitress was super fast.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep3Ai: The AI proposes a ready-to-paste version: same words, nothing invented, discreet local SEO. The client has the final say: they can request as many edits as they want — text, voice, or photos — until full validation.
defiReputexa.journeyStep3ChallengeNote: With the challenge, we naturally encourage keeping the mention of the person cited if the client has already written it.
defiReputexa.journeyStep4b: Button type ‘Publish my review in 2 clicks’ to Google (or elsewhere).
defiReputexa.journeyStep5Msg: ‘A huge thank you [First Name]! … As promised on the poster at the counter, your number will no longer be used for this process… archiving / deletion within 120 days… See you soon at [Establishment]! 👋’
defiReputexa.journeyRecapBody: The final ranking, the reminder of the rewards you entered, and the archivable history: everything is grouped on this page for your team and the public link (team).

reviewBoost.title: A Google Review for REPUTEXA
reviewBoost.description: As simple as a WhatsApp request: you write, the AI can refine the text, then you publish on Google. The stars are chosen on Google, not here. Once finished, this block will no longer reappear.
reviewBoost.step1: Write a few words about your experience with REPUTEXA.
reviewBoost.step2: Optional: tap ‘Optimize with AI’ for a ready-to-paste text.
reviewBoost.step3: ‘Publish on Google’ saves your testimony, copies the text to the clipboard, and opens the review page in a new tab — choose the stars, paste, send.
reviewBoost.placeholder: E.g.: time savings, AI responses, Google visibility…
reviewBoost.hintFooter: Your review may be reviewed before appearing on our site. You will not see this section after publication.
reviewBoost.toastTooShort: Write at least a few words.
reviewBoost.toastOptimized: Text optimized — please review and adjust if needed.
reviewBoost.toastPublishDone: Text copied — the Google page opens in a new tab. Choose your stars, paste, send.
reviewBoost.toastPublishDoneNoTab: Saved — text copied. Paste it on the REPUTEXA Google listing.
reviewBoost.toastClipboardFail: Clipboard copy failed: select the text and copy it (Ctrl+C).

reviewCollection.pageSubtitle: Automate WhatsApp review requests after each sale — Zenith Plan.
reviewCollection.paywallBody: Automatic review collection via WhatsApp (POS connection, courtesy window, GDPR) is exclusively available with the Zenith Plan.
reviewCollection.section1Intro: Define the ideal delay before a message is sent to your clients after their visit.
reviewCollection.activityProfileExampleCaption: Typical send window for this profile:
reviewCollection.physicalDelayExplainer: Usually the message goes out about {delay} after the visit, with slight random variation so it feels natural.
reviewCollection.businessTypeHint: Type of operation recorded with the backup:
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyIntro: REPUTEXA monitors the tracking number. The countdown only starts when the package is officially delivered (carrier scan).
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyImmediate: Immediate pleasure (clothes, gadgets) — send 2h after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyTestMount: Test & assembly (furniture, high-tech) — send 24h after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyCustom: Custom — choose the delay after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxAfterDelivery: Reviews after delivery: the webhook must send status (e.g. Delivered) and whatsapp_consent: true once the package is delivered.
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSend2h: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp 2h after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSend24h: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp 24h after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSendCustom: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp {delay} after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxShippedOnly: If status = shipped only, nothing is scheduled until it is delivered.
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxRich: <lead>Reviews after delivery</lead>: the webhook must send <mono>status</mono> (e.g. Delivered) and <mono>whatsapp_consent: true</mono> once the package is delivered.
reviewCollection.toastTokenOk: Legacy token regenerated. Update your POS if you are still using this method.
reviewCollection.toastApiKeyOk: New key generated. Update your POS configuration right away.

custom.label: Custom (free-form delay)
custom.example: You set the exact delay in minutes

reviewCollection.rgpdSectionIntro: For online stores, you must obtain explicit customer consent before sending any WhatsApp message. Use the text below on your checkout flow.
reviewCollection.rgpdConsentBody: I agree to receive a single WhatsApp invitation from {establishment} through our partner REPUTEXA to leave a review after I receive my package.
reviewCollection.rgpdShopifyLi4: In the <bold>“Opt-in label”</bold> field, paste the REPUTEXA text (copied above).
reviewCollection.rgpdStripeLi1: In your Stripe Dashboard, open <bold>Checkout & Payment Links</bold>.
reviewCollection.rgpdStripeLi4: Set the label text to the <bold>REPUTEXA consent copy</bold> (copied above).
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseHeading: Clause for your terms / privacy policy
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseInstruction: <bold>Note:</bold> Copy the clause below into your terms, replacing “Article X” with the correct section number.
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseBody: Article [X] – Loyalty, reviews, and customer satisfaction: we use REPUTEXA to run our loyalty program (points, rewards, benefit-related messages) and, where applicable, to ask for a review after a visit or order. The data needed (identity, phone, purchase context, point balance if any) is processed in line with our privacy policy. REPUTEXA acts as a technical processor on our instructions; AI-generated content is suggested and we approve it before sending. Points have no cash value. When importing customer files, we confirm we have a lawful basis. Replying STOP ends messages; a full opt-out may close the loyalty account under our rules.
reviewCollection.rgpdNoteBody: Including this clause documents your duty to inform (loyalty and reviews). If you do not add or apply it on your site and documents, compliance is solely your responsibility; REPUTEXA cannot be liable for gaps in notice or consent.
reviewCollection.certificateTip: Summary document for your records (customers, insurer, authority): current published legal version, latest Guardian cycle, account commitment — REPUTEXA timestamp.
reviewCollection.section2IntroPhysical: Share these details with your POS vendor to automate sending.
reviewCollection.section2IntroEcommerce: Give this link to your integrator or automation tool (Zapier, Make, etc.) to trigger after delivery.
reviewCollection.webhookRotateWarning: ⚠️ Rotating the key revokes the old URL — update your POS / Zapier immediately.
reviewCollection.noApiKey: No API key has been generated for this account.
reviewCollection.rotateKeyTitle: Generate a new key (revokes the old URL immediately)
reviewCollection.zapierHint: Your POS does not support direct webhooks?
reviewCollection.posterA3: A3 — wall / readable from a distance
reviewCollection.posterTip: Tip: A4 at the counter; A5 when space is tight; A3 for wall displays. Print at 100% scale without “Fit to page”.
reviewCollection.ecommerceCheckoutNote: For online stores, actually inserting the clause (section 1) and applying it in your checkout is your responsibility. Without implementation on your site, REPUTEXA cannot be liable for gaps in notice or consent.
reviewCollection.payloadNoAuthNote: No authentication header required — the key is in the URL.
reviewCollection.ecomCardIdentityBody: first_name and phone — required for WhatsApp.
reviewCollection.ecomCardComplianceBody: whatsapp_consent must be true to allow sending.
reviewCollection.ecomCardLogisticsBody: status triggers send once the package is marked delivered (e.g. Delivered, Zugestellt…) — tracking_number optional.
reviewCollection.webhookPhysicalPayloadExplainer: For brick-and-mortar, send this JSON from your POS via webhook: first name, phone, and optionally a purchase label to personalize the WhatsApp invite. Customers can also enroll via your public page (QR) and add their Wallet pass (Apple or Google depending on device).
reviewCollection.statGeneratedSub: Polished copy published by the customer
reviewCollection.auditTitle: Log — where did the invite come from?
reviewCollection.auditIntro: Each row shows the entry path (API key webhook or Zenith), received date, known context, and status. Phone partially masked here; full detail (e.g. IP) is in the GDPR export (Settings → Download my data).
reviewCollection.auditRetention: Queue identifiers are anonymized or deleted after 120 days at the latest (aggregated stats may be kept). A new WhatsApp invite for the same number is not sent until 120 days after the previous campaign started; after that, a new send is possible unless opted out (STOP).

zapier.title: Connect with Zapier
zapier.subtitle: 6,000+ apps — no code required
zapier.t2a: Search for “Webhooks by Zapier” in apps
zapier.t2c: Zapier gives you a link — copy it into your software as the send URL
zapier.t3a: Search again for “Webhooks by Zapier” under actions
zapier.t3c: Copy your REPUTEXA link below, then fill the table field by field
zapier.ecoStatus: Delivery status — send schedules when Delivered
zapier.ecoQueueNote: Until the package is delivered (Delivered), nothing is queued.
zapier.reassurance: If you see other options, ignore them — your setup is correct.
zapier.footerTeam: Our technical team is here to help.

reviewCollection.ecommerceModalTitle: Sending WhatsApp in e-commerce requires adding the Article X clause to your terms and a checkbox at checkout. Have you configured your checkout flow?
reviewCollection.ecommerceModalWarning: Running campaigns without these legal elements is entirely your responsibility. If the clause and checkbox are not actually on your site, REPUTEXA cannot be liable for notice or consent gaps.
reviewCollection.pageFooterLegal: REPUTEXA is a technical processor. Using this service means you accept our Terms and Privacy Policy (loyalty & reviews); obtaining consent and informing end customers is the controller’s duty (merchant).

llmBudget.title: AI usage safeguard
llmBudget.subtitle: Today’s counter (UTC, resets at midnight UTC)
llmBudget.explanation: Each AI-assisted reply, summary, or automated message counts as one use. While you stay under the first threshold, responsiveness stays at full speed. Beyond that, the service may slow slightly to protect your budget (and ours). At the hard cap, new AI actions pause until the next UTC day — so keep an eye on this gauge.
llmBudget.statusOk: You’re in the comfort zone — everything is smooth.
llmBudget.statusSoft: Past the comfort threshold: you may notice slight throttling.
llmBudget.statusHard: Cap reached: AI actions resume after midnight UTC.

Bounds: at least ~30 s in the future, max ~12 months — like scheduled email sends, unrelated to any 30-day legal notice.

reviewsPage.pageSubtitle: Manage reviews and the automatic publishing queue.
reviewsPage.queueSectionDesc: Queue: replies ready or awaiting validation. Human pacing before they go live; you can edit, reschedule, or cancel.
reviewsPage.queueEmpty: No reviews in the automatic queue.
reviewsPage.publishedDesc: Replies marked published (including after the queue).
reviewsPage.publishedEmpty: No published replies for this period / location.
reviewsPage.explainerItem1: Imported reviews (Google, etc.) show up here as soon as they arrive.
reviewsPage.explainerItem2: 4–5 stars: an AI reply is generated and queued for automatic publishing after a few hours.
reviewsPage.explainerItem3: 1–3 stars — Vision: you validate or edit the suggestion on this tab. Pulse & Zenith: you also get a WhatsApp alert (review + suggestion) to react on your phone.
reviewsPage.explainerItem4: All replies go through a queue (~2–7 hours) for believable timing. If several reviews arrive at once, sends are spaced automatically (a few minutes apart).
reviewsPage.explainerItem5: While queued, you can edit the text, delay publishing (+1h or +2h), or cancel the send.
reviewsPage.explainerItem6: After publishing, the review appears under “Published”.
reviewsPage.searchReviewsPlaceholder: Search by customer name or review excerpt…

updatesModal.title: What’s new in REPUTEXA?

alertsPage.pageSubtitle: Negative reviews (AI reply to validate) and Pulse/Zenith toxic shield — Vision: no WhatsApp alert on bad reviews; everything happens here.
alertsPage.sectionNegativeDesc: Edit if needed, then queue: automatic publishing after a few hours (human-like pacing), same as positive reviews.
alertsPage.emptyDesc: No negative reviews pending and no active toxic threats.

alertsPage.legalComplaintTemplate:
Subject: Request to remove non-compliant review

To whom it may concern,

We are writing regarding a review published on your platform under the name {reviewerName}, which appears to violate your current terms of use.

The review contains hateful, defamatory, and/or demonstrably false statements that seriously harm our professional reputation and the integrity of our business.

Under your content policy and applicable law, we ask that you remove this review without undue delay.

We remain available for any further information.

Best regards,
REPUTEXA Shield team

bananoWalletDesigner.panelLead: iPhone-style preview: colours, logo, and the same HD strip crop as the .pkpass file. Injected preview CSS is not exported to the pass.
bananoWalletDesigner.sectionInspirationLead: Full-bleed SMB themes — one click applies palette, illustration, strip, and light; fine‑tune, then save.
bananoWalletDesigner.themeIllustrationHint: One HD image feeds both the PassKit strip and this preview; the glass header and gradients keep titles and points readable.
bananoWalletDesigner.sectionAiLead: Describe your business (sector, style, colours). The AI picks the trade illustration, colours, and preview CSS (no AI-generated bitmaps — bundled SVG backgrounds).

bananoParametres.relancesTabsLead: Four scenarios in the tabs below: win-back (inactive clients), new member welcome, VIP of the month, and birthdays.

bananoParametres.segmentPrivilegeLead: Top spender / visits for the previous calendar month.
bananoParametres.segmentBirthdayLead: Birthday: on-the-day message and advance reminder.
bananoParametres.whatsappHelpA: Unchecked = no automatic send for this rule (the cron skips the row). You only write the personal middle line. The first name comes from the customer record, and the intro uses the business name saved for your store:
bananoParametres.whatsappHelpC: . Also set birthday dates on records (Customer base). VIP of the month uses the previous calendar month; automatic send on the 1st (Europe/Paris) when the rule is on. Delivery: cron + Twilio. Welcome: J+N after the loyalty record is created (Paris).
bananoParametres.establishmentNameFallback: not set — “our team” will be used when sending

bananoGhostTokens.title: Terminal & Agents — Sentinel Ghost
bananoGhostTokens.intro: Each Windows POS PC gets a secret Bearer token. Paste it into the Ghost Agent to resolve REP-… scans (customers and staff with staff credit), VCHR-… vouchers, and transactions. The secret is shown only once.
bananoGhostTokens.testMacroRest: Windows PC only — in Ghost Agent, enter a dummy amount then “Test SendInput” to see how your software reacts to injected keystrokes (without touching loyalty data in the database).
bananoGhostTokens.empty: No agents yet. Create a token to connect the Windows checkout PC.
bananoGhostTokens.dialogHint: After you confirm, a secret token will show once. Copy it into the Reputexa Ghost app (Bearer token field).
bananoGhostTokens.secretBody: For security it will never be shown again. Only a hash is stored — if you lose it, revoke access and create a new agent.
bananoGhostTokens.toastCreateOk: Agent created — copy the token now
bananoGhostTokens.confirmRevoke: Revoke access “{label}”? The Windows app will no longer be able to call the API with this token.

bananoComptaExpert.lockedBody: This module (monthly loyalty + staff summary, PDF and CSV exports for your accounting team) is included in the {plan} plan. Track every voucher with date, beneficiary, and amount — no more “invisible” gaps between POS and perks.
bananoComptaExpert.title: Accountant — supporting documents
bananoComptaExpert.intro: Totals for loyalty discounts (vouchers redeemed) and staff perks actually consumed at the POS. Share with your accountant alongside your ledgers — same idea as a marketing spend report or tracked in-kind benefits. Pro tip: add a REPUTEXA tender or discount line in your POS for painless reconciliation.
bananoComptaExpert.loyaltySub: Fixed-amount vouchers (€). Excluding percent: {percentCount} % voucher(s) · label-only: {labelOnlyCount}. Total redemptions: {redemptions}.
bananoComptaExpert.staffSub: Sum of debits recorded at the POS for the period. Movements: {debitEventCount}.
bananoComptaExpert.footnote: This document is a summary supporting record for loyalty marketing discounts and staff in-kind benefits logged through REPUTEXA; it complements your POS records and does not replace legal or tax filing duties.

bananoSentinel.explainerBody: It is the live "Ghost" event feed: loyalty card scans, enrollments, credits or debits through the Windows agent or integrations, voucher redemptions, device links. Use it to check in real time that pass-throughs and the Wallet behave correctly. It is not the hourly cash flow chart; that one is under Analytics - Cash flow.
bananoSentinel.title: Sentinel Live Feed
bananoSentinel.subtitle: Live stream of Ghost Agent scans and transactions (Supabase Realtime).
bananoSentinel.empty: No Ghost events yet. Scans from the Windows agent will show here.
