bananoTerminal.degradedStale: At least one action hasn’t synced in over 24 hours — check checkout or remove duplicates if you already entered everything manually.
bananoTerminal.birthdayAfterPrompt: Enter the ticket total (incl. tax) below to show the discounted price.
bananoTerminal.vouchersSectionHint: Loyalty: single-use voucher. Staff: euro balance decreases until zero. Tap a voucher to prepare it, scan the QR or enter the code. Validity: see each line.
bananoTerminal.voucherNoActive: No active voucher for this customer.
bananoTerminal.hintNameCaisse: LAST then FIRST, no accents — e.g. MOREAU CLEMENCE or SMITH JOHN
bananoTerminal.hintPhoneSearch: Phone search when at least 3 digits are entered
bananoTerminal.placeholderNewName: Last name then first name (e.g. SMITH JOHN)
bananoTerminal.selectClientPrompt: Select or create a customer to see balance and redeem.
bananoTerminal.birthdayFallbackLine: Remember your personalized greeting at checkout.
bananoTerminal.voucherAtTillSub: {count, plural, one {# active voucher — select it below to redeem.} other {# active vouchers — select them below to redeem.}}
bananoTerminal.titleEarnPoints: Enter a valid incl.-tax total to credit points.
bananoTerminal.titleEarnStamps: Enter a valid incl.-tax total to credit stamps (€ rate configured).
bananoTerminal.settingsBannerRest: (points per visit, threshold, reward, WhatsApp reminders, birthday): tab
bananoTerminal.settingsBannerEnd: on this Review WhatsApp loyalty page.
bananoTerminal.terminalRegisterHint: Must match the shift/register ID in Reputexa Sync to link till tickets and Wallet scans (±60 s window).
bananoTerminal.tabletFullscreenHint: Tablet view: add the page to your home screen for full screen (no browser bar).
bananoTerminal.terminalSearchBlurb: Search by phone or name, credit after purchase, redeem rewards. Open full-screen terminal mode for checkout / staff (button below).
bananoTerminal.timelineEmpty: No activity on this profile yet.
bananoTerminal.terminalFsUniqueBody: The URL contains a code specific to your business (after /terminal/). Multiple cashiers: no problem. Two REPUTEXA businesses: never the same link.
bananoTerminal.terminalFsConv1: Last name then first name (e.g. SMITH JOHN).
bananoTerminal.terminalFsConv2: Uppercase, no accents; apostrophe → space (O BRIEN LIAM).
bananoTerminal.terminalFsConv3: On save: automatic normalization — verify before confirming.
bananoTerminal.terminalDashUniqueBody: The terminal URL contains a code specific to your business (after /terminal/). Multiple cashiers or one in-store tablet: no problem. Different REPUTEXA customers never use the same link.
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv1: Always last name first, then first name (e.g. SMITH JOHN).
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv2: Uppercase, no accents, no apostrophe (use a space: O BRIEN LIAM).
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv3: On save: automatic normalization (accents, apostrophes, case). Verify before confirming.
bananoTerminal.pointsRateFootnote: {rate} pt per € incl. tax (total rate{bonusPart}), rounded up: e.g. {exampleGross} → {examplePts} pts.
bananoTerminal.stampsRateFootnote: {rate} {unitRate} per € incl. tax, rounded up{visitPart}. E.g. {exampleGross} → {exampleStamps} {unitEx}{bonusVisitPart}.
bananoTerminal.bonusActivePointsEuro: Bonus active: +{extra} pt/€ on the rate (total {total} pt/€, rounded up on incl.-tax amount).
bananoTerminal.bonusInactivePointsEuro: +{extra} pt/€ bonus saved, but outside the period{range}. Only the base rate applies today.
bananoTerminal.bonusActivePointsVisit: {n, plural, one {Bonus active: +# flat point per purchase (legacy mode).} other {Bonus active: +# flat points per purchase (legacy mode).}}
bananoTerminal.bonusActiveStampsEuro: Bonus active: +{extra} stamp(s)/€ on the rate (total {total}/€).
bananoTerminal.bonusInactiveStampsEuro: Bonus stamps/€ saved, but outside the period{rangeShort}.
bananoTerminal.bonusActiveStampsVisit: {n, plural, one {Bonus active: +# flat stamp per purchase (legacy mode).} other {Bonus active: +# flat stamps per purchase (legacy mode).}}
bananoTerminal.staffVoucherIntro: Available balance: {balance}. Enter the amount to apply to this ticket (max. balance or entered gross).
bananoTerminal.loyaltyVoucherOneShot: This loyalty voucher will be marked as used (single use). Make sure the discount or benefit shown is applied to the check.

bananoLoyaltySettings.toastCustomCreated: Custom event created — edit the name and dates, then “Apply”.
bananoLoyaltySettings.toastEndAfterStart: End must be on or after start.
bananoLoyaltySettings.toastBonusDatesSaved: Bonus dates saved in the form.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errThresholdPoints: Points program: threshold must be at least 1.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errThresholdStamps: Stamps program: threshold must be at least 1.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errPointsPerEuro: Enter how many points per € incl. tax for the points program (e.g. 1).
bananoLoyaltySettings.errStampsProgram: Stamps program: enter either stamps per € incl. tax or a per-purchase flat amount (> 0).
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusPointsNeedEnable: Check “Enable bonus” and save with dates — otherwise “bonus points per €” won’t apply at checkout.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusStampsNeedEnable: Check “Enable bonus” with dates — otherwise “bonus stamps per €” won’t apply.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusNeedDates: Bonus: enter start and end dates.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusEndBeforeStart: End of period must be on or after start.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusPointsPerEuro: Points mode: enter bonus points per € incl. tax (> 0) — they add to the base rate (e.g. 1 + 1 = 2 pt/€).
bananoLoyaltySettings.errBonusStampsPerEuro: Stamps mode: enter bonus stamps per € incl. tax (> 0) for the period.
bananoLoyaltySettings.errVoucherValidity: Voucher validity: 1 to 3650 days, or leave blank.
bananoLoyaltySettings.toastSavedSuccess: Program saved — the terminal updates live.
bananoLoyaltySettings.voucherTitle: Threshold gift voucher ({unit} · single use, archived, QR at checkout)
bananoLoyaltySettings.voucherIntro: Each checkout visit, when {unit} total exceeds the goal above, a voucher is created automatically and the customer keeps the {unit} remainder. The benefit is redeemed only with the voucher code (or QR) — no manual punch-card debit.
bananoLoyaltySettings.voucherNatureLabel: Benefit type shown on the voucher
bananoLoyaltySettings.validityDaysLabel: Validity period (days after issue; blank = no limit)
bananoLoyaltySettings.whatsappCheckbox: Automatically send the voucher code by WhatsApp when created (customer phone required).
bananoLoyaltySettings.modePointsHelp: Checkout credits points from ticket amount (€ × rate, rounded up) plus any bonus.
bananoLoyaltySettings.modeStampsHelp: Checkout credits stamps: from the ticket (€ × rate), per-purchase flat, or both.
bananoLoyaltySettings.goalPointsLabel: Goal: how many points to unlock the reward?
bananoLoyaltySettings.goalStampsLabel: Goal: how many stamps to unlock the reward?
bananoLoyaltySettings.rewardLabelPoints: Label on voucher and checkout screen
bananoLoyaltySettings.rewardLabelStamps: Label on voucher and checkout screen (e.g. 1 free menu, -15%…)
bananoLoyaltySettings.creditPointsLabel: Credit: 1 € incl. tax = how many points? (required · rounded up on ticket)
bananoLoyaltySettings.stampsPerEuroLabel: Stamps per € incl. tax (0 = ignore amount: use only the flat per purchase below)
bananoLoyaltySettings.stampsPerEuroHelpA: Rounded up on incl.-tax amount. E.g. rate
bananoLoyaltySettings.stampsPerEuroHelpC: {count, plural, one {# stamp} other {# stamps}}. Leave 0 if you only want a fixed flat per purchase.
bananoLoyaltySettings.stampsFlatLabel: Flat stamps per purchase (adds to ticket amount if both are used)
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapCashSubtitle: Single round-up on incl.-tax total × combined rate (base ± bonus per €).
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapRewardLine: {reward}: from {threshold} {threshold, plural, one {point} other {points}} → threshold voucher “{text}”
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapOffBonusRest: (dates not covered or bonus off): {rate} pt/€ — e.g. €1.20 → {exA} pts · €50.01 → {exB} pts.
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapBonusPeriod: Bonus period {start} to {end} (Europe/Paris, inclusive): combined rate {rate} pt/€ ({base} base + {bonus} bonus) — e.g. €1.20 → {exA} pts · €50.01 → {exB} pts.
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapBonusHintIncomplete: Enter dates and “bonus points per €” below to show the rate during the event here.
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapStampsCardLine: {carte} at checkout, once {threshold} {threshold, plural, one {stamp} other {stamps}} are earned, a {bon} (code + QR) is created; the customer keeps the stamp remainder. The reward is redeemed like in points mode (voucher validation).
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapStampsDisplayLine: {affichage}: “{text}” (and % / € benefit if set on the stamp voucher).
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapBonusStampsLine: Bonus period {start} to {end}: combined rate {rate} — e.g. €24.90 → {ex}{flat}
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapStampsBonusHint: Enter dates + bonus stamps / € to see the combined rate during the event.
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapFlatOnlyLine: {forfait}: +{n} {n, plural, one {stamp} other {stamps}} (no ticket amount). Optional bonus: see settings below.
bananoLoyaltySettings.recapDefineRateOrFlat: Set a rate per € or a flat per purchase in the form above.
bananoLoyaltySettings.bonusDetailsLead: Bonus adds a {strong} to the program (e.g. base 1 pt/€ + 1 pt/€ bonus = {rate2}, single round-up on total: €50.01 → 101 pts).
bananoLoyaltySettings.legacyFlatWarning: Legacy setting detected: flat +{n} pt/purchase. Save with the new “per euro” field to migrate — flat will stop being used.
bananoLoyaltySettings.presetDblClickTitle: Double-click to change dates before applying
bananoLoyaltySettings.userPresetDblClickTitle: Double-click to rename or change dates
bananoLoyaltySettings.enableBonusCheckbox: Enable bonus on the dates below
bananoLoyaltySettings.bonusPointsPerEuroLabel: Bonus points per € incl. tax (add to base rate for the period)
bananoLoyaltySettings.bonusPointsPlaceholder: e.g. 1 to double a 1 pt/€ base rate
bananoLoyaltySettings.bonusStampsPerEuroLabel: Bonus stamps per € incl. tax (add to stamps/€ rate for the period)
bananoLoyaltySettings.seeGreenRecapExamples: on each ticket (examples in the green recap).
bananoLoyaltySettings.todayOutsideWindow: This day is outside the bonus window (Paris) — see below.
bananoLoyaltySettings.todaySeeGreenRecap: For bonus dates, see the green recap.
bananoLoyaltySettings.todayWindowLine: Window {start} → {end} (inclusive, Paris): today checkout applies {rate} only (without the +{bonus} bonus).
bananoLoyaltySettings.stampsTodaySeeGreen: — period detail in the green recap.
bananoLoyaltySettings.flatPreview: {flat}: +{n} {n, plural, one {stamp} other {stamps}} / purchase (no ticket amount).
bananoLoyaltySettings.signupWelcomeIntro: Si vous l’activez, un bon unique est créé à la première inscription (QR public, Wallet OAuth ou fiche créée en caisse). Booste l’adhésion en montrant un bénéfice immédiat.
bananoLoyaltySettings.signupWelcomeEnable: Offrir un avantage automatique à l’inscription
bananoLoyaltySettings.signupWelcomeValidityLabel: Validité (jours après émission ; vide = sans limite)
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncSectionLead: Recevez les tickets de caisse dans le pilotage via l'agent Reputexa Sync (Windows). La clé sert uniquement à authentifier l'agent sur votre compte.
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncSecretLabel: Clé secrète (à coller dans Reputexa Sync)
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncCopyErr: Copie impossible. Sélectionnez la clé manuellement.
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncDownloadPlaceholderToast: No public installer is configured. For local use: open the reputexa-sync folder in this repo, run npm install && npm start (Electron). To enable this button, set NEXT_PUBLIC_REPUTEXA_SYNC_WINDOWS_URL to your hosted .exe (CDN, GitHub Releases, etc.).
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncStatusActiveWithTerminals: 🟢 {count, plural, one {# register connected} other {# registers connected}}
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncStatusAriaActive: Synchronisation caisse active (réception dans les dernières 24 heures)
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncStatusAriaIdle: Aucune réception caisse dans les dernières 24 heures
bananoLoyaltySettings.cashSyncActivatedToast: Liaison caisse activée. Configurez l'agent avec la clé affichée.

bananoEliteRewardSettings.title: Elite reward (Top Clients)
bananoEliteRewardSettings.intro: When enabled, each send from Champions Circle first creates a loyalty voucher (default amount below), then the WhatsApp message. The Wallet link lets customers add the pass. One voucher per customer per ranking month.
bananoEliteRewardSettings.enabledLabel: Create a € voucher and include the Wallet link in the Elite message
bananoEliteRewardSettings.validityLabel: Voucher validity (days after issue, empty = no expiry)
bananoEliteRewardSettings.templateLabel: Top Clients WhatsApp template (empty = automatic text + Wallet link)
bananoEliteRewardSettings.variablesHelp: Variables: {customer_name} {reward_amount} {wallet_link} {offer_text} {establishment_name} {voucher_code} {month_key}. If the Wallet link is missing, a line is added automatically.
bananoEliteRewardSettings.disabledHint: Disabled: Elite sends use only the usual offer text (no voucher or Wallet link).
bananoEliteRewardSettings.errAmount: Enter an amount greater than €0 when the option is enabled.

bananoStaffSettings.errPinDigits: The new code must be exactly 4 digits.
bananoStaffSettings.toastPinUpdated: Staff PIN updated — share it with the person.
bananoStaffSettings.errLoadAllowance: Could not load staff voucher settings.
bananoStaffSettings.errStaffAllowanceAmount: Staff voucher: enter a monthly amount greater than 0.
bananoStaffSettings.errStaffAllowanceValidity: Staff voucher validity: 1 to 3650 days or blank.
bananoStaffSettings.errPin4: The code must be exactly 4 digits.
bananoStaffSettings.toastStaffAdded: Staff member added — the code appears next to the name.
bananoStaffSettings.toastAccessCut: Terminal access removed for this staff member
bananoStaffSettings.sectionTitle: Checkout PIN, client records and vouchers
bananoStaffSettings.sectionLead: Each staff member is linked to a client record. Their 4-digit PIN appears at the terminal; two people cannot share the same code.
bananoStaffSettings.allowanceCheckbox: Enable automatic monthly generation (1st-of-month cron · amount below).
bananoStaffSettings.allowanceDisabledHint: While unchecked, no amount or recipient list is shown here. Staff are still managed below.
bananoStaffSettings.allowanceEnabledLead: A euro voucher is issued each month for everyone already registered as staff (customer profile + checkout code). Balance decreases at checkout. Reporting and fixes: Customer base → Staff voucher archive tab.
bananoStaffSettings.allowanceMonthlyLabel: Amount loaded per voucher / month / person (€)
bananoStaffSettings.allowanceValidityLabel: Validity after issue (days; blank = no limit)
bananoStaffSettings.recipientsHint: Same list as staff with a customer profile: remove voucher eligibility here without removing the checkout code.
bananoStaffSettings.addStaffHintEmpty: Add a staff member below (customer profile + PIN).
bananoStaffSettings.newStaffLead: Search an existing customer profile (name or phone), select it, then assign the 4-digit code.
bananoStaffSettings.emptyStaff: No staff yet. Full-screen terminal stays open without a staff picker until the list has entries.
bananoStaffSettings.deleteLead: This is permanent. Related history will no longer show a staff name.

bananoOmnipresent.forecastUnavailable: Forecast not available in this version.
bananoOmnipresent.toastWaSent: {count, plural, one {WhatsApp: # message sent.} other {WhatsApp: # messages sent.}}
bananoOmnipresent.toastWaSkippedDesc: {skipped, plural, one {# contact skipped (messaged within the last 30 days).} other {# contacts skipped (messaged within the last 30 days).}}
bananoOmnipresent.toastWaAllSkipped: Nothing sent: all eligible contacts were already messaged recently (30 days).
bananoOmnipresent.toastWaFailed: WhatsApp send failed. Check your Twilio setup and phone numbers.
bananoOmnipresent.toastWaNoTargets: No customers match the current criteria.
bananoOmnipresent.title: Omnipresent dashboard
bananoOmnipresent.subtitle: Figures and text from your loyalty visits, amounts entered at checkout, “lost customer” follow-up rules, and purchase notes. Connect catalog / inventory to enrich product cards.
bananoOmnipresent.cashVsLoyaltyTitle: Comparatif CA du mois : caisse globale vs fidélité
bananoOmnipresent.cashVsLoyaltyHint: CA Global (Caisse) : somme des tickets reçus via l'agent sync. CA Fidélité (Reputexa) : montants saisis sur les passages carte fidélité.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionOverviewDesc: Day / week / month KPIs, revenue goal, champions.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionTeamDesc: Per-staff metrics, conversion and reviews for the selected period.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionActivityDesc: Live event log and monthly CSV exports.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionCommerceDesc: Programme KPIs, day / week / month targets and daily register activity.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionRetentionDesc: Loyalty funnel and weekday traffic (last 7 days).
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionReportsDesc: AI monthly PDFs, voucher archives and accountant hand-off.
bananoOmnipresent.overviewTemporalStripSubtitle: Day, week, month — numbers and insight at a glance.
bananoOmnipresent.loyaltyProfitabilityTitle: Bilan valeur fidélité — mois en cours
bananoOmnipresent.loyaltyProfitabilityRoi: CA par € de remise fixe utilisée : ×{ratio}
bananoOmnipresent.loyaltyProfitabilityRoiEmpty: Ratio : en attente (pas encore de bon € utilisé ce mois).
bananoOmnipresent.overviewLoyaltyPulseTitle: Loyalty programme — day / week / month pulse
bananoOmnipresent.overviewWeekdayEmpty: No distribution for this period yet.
bananoOmnipresent.overviewWeekdayInPdfHint: Weekday traffic: see the monthly PDF (pp. 3–4).
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionOpsDesc: Calendar (same month as Elite KPIs), checkout quality, automations.
bananoOmnipresent.pilotageSectionCashSyncDesc: Tickets received via the Reputexa Sync agent, broken down by register (terminal) and staff name entered at shift start. Capture rate is the share of tickets linked to a Wallet member (±60 s window, same terminal).
bananoOmnipresent.cashTerminalTableEmpty: No synced tickets this month. Check the agent and API key.
bananoOmnipresent.cashStaffPerformanceEmpty: No staff rows for this month.
bananoOmnipresent.opsCalendarSubtitle: Monthly view — same month as Elite KPIs. Toggle visits or stamps.
bananoOmnipresent.opsCalendarLegend: Shade reflects volume for the month ({mode}). Hover a cell for detail.
bananoOmnipresent.opsCashSubtitle: Data from loyalty terminal checkouts. Without a full POS feed, revenue shown is from card-identified tickets with amounts entered.
bananoOmnipresent.opsCashWalletHint: Sum of ticket totals entered for the calendar month on display.
bananoOmnipresent.opsBasketNeedData: Add more ticket amounts in the period to refine the comparison.
bananoOmnipresent.opsAutoSubtitle: Automatic sends and estimated Wallet / WhatsApp campaign impact.
bananoOmnipresent.opsAutoBirthdayHint: Successful “birthday day” WhatsApp sends today (merchant time zone).
bananoOmnipresent.opsAutoPushSends: {n, plural, one {# send counted this month} other {# sends counted this month}}
bananoOmnipresent.opsAutoRelancesDetailIntro: Same month as above. If a rule is unchecked under Settings - Follow-ups, the cron skips it. Totals = successful sends (logs).
bananoOmnipresent.opsAutoSendsThisMonth: {n, plural, one {# send this month} other {# sends this month}}
bananoOmnipresent.feedSubQrSignup: Counter QR, checkout or signup link
bananoOmnipresent.eliteDeckAria: Elite command — KPIs, retention, live activity
bananoOmnipresent.eliteKpiCaHint: Incl.-tax total of card-identified checkouts for the displayed month.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteKpiBasketHint: Average ticket when amounts are entered at checkout.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteAtRiskSubtitle: No visit for more than {days} days.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteAtRiskEmpty: No inactive members match for now.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteLiveSubtitle: Loyalty log events — live updates when realtime is available.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteKpiCaTooltip: Sum of gross amounts entered on card checkouts (points or stamps) for the month shown in the pilotage calendar.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteKpiFreqTooltip: Card visits ÷ distinct members with at least one visit in that period.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteKpiBasketTooltip: Wallet revenue (sum of entered amounts) ÷ visits where a gross amount was entered.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteAtRiskAllActive: Great news — every member is still active!
bananoOmnipresent.eliteAtRiskAllActiveHint: No one has crossed the inactivity threshold.
bananoOmnipresent.eliteWaPrefill:
Hello {customer},

{establishment} here — we’ve been thinking of you and would love to see you again soon. Hope to see you!
bananoOmnipresent.commercialHeroChampionsEmpty: Not enough data to show champion cards.
bananoOmnipresent.commercialHeroRelancesHint: Birthdays and inactive customers: configure messages under Automations below.
bananoOmnipresent.staffReputexaLine: REPUTEXA shows which employee is most effective at bringing in loyal customers.
bananoOmnipresent.staffHelp: Calendar-month data (each new month, counters reset for the current month). Profiles created in the period, revenue from ticket amounts at checkout, Google 4–5★ reviews tied to checkouts by staff member (estimate). The monthly PDF report uses the same aggregates with retention and management analysis.
bananoOmnipresent.staffGoogleDisclaimer: Google reviews are matched when the name on the review matches a profile with at least one visit credited by that staff member in the period (heuristic).
bananoOmnipresent.staffEmpty: Add staff under WhatsApp Reviews → Settings to enable the staff picker and these metrics.
bananoOmnipresent.transformFormula: Conversion rate = profiles created ÷ tickets checked out.
bananoOmnipresent.staffPageInfo: Page {current} / {total} — {n} {n, plural, one {staff member} other {staff members}}
bananoOmnipresent.activityHelp: Each row shows the staff member logged into the terminal: checkouts, profile creation, etc.
bananoOmnipresent.activityFeedGroupVouchersStaff: {count, plural, one {# voucher redeemed by {staff}} other {# vouchers redeemed by {staff}}}
bananoOmnipresent.activityFeedGroupVouchersClient: {count, plural, one {# voucher redeemed — {client}} other {# vouchers redeemed — {client}}}
bananoOmnipresent.traceabilityCsvHelp: Calendar-month export (all rows in the month), like the AI report: one file per month, one click to download. No automatic PDF yet — the CSV includes date, customer, event type, and staff member.
bananoOmnipresent.loyaltyProgramHelp: Points issued, stamps credited, and vouchers generated — from checkout (today, ISO week, and month to date).
bananoOmnipresent.cashDailySubtitle: One row per calendar day. Figures come only from loyalty-terminal logs.
bananoOmnipresent.cashHelp: Last {days} calendar days: visits logged at checkout, cumulative revenue (incl.-tax amounts entered), average ticket on rows with an amount, item total if you use the optional terminal field, and most common purchase notes (a stand-in for “how many of each” until a product catalog is connected).
bananoOmnipresent.retentionHelp: Funnel for the current calendar month (new customers, returns, VIP). Right: checkout intensity over the last 7 days by weekday (green = more activity).
bananoOmnipresent.clickRowDetail: Click a row for detail and charts.
bananoOmnipresent.smartCardsHelp: Signals from your data; bestseller and stock once ERP / catalog is connected.
bananoOmnipresent.noTicketAmounts: No incl.-tax amounts in the data yet: enter ticket totals at checkout to enable revenue, average ticket, and VIP comparisons.
bananoOmnipresent.reportHelp: Closed month: PDF (AI, figures, D/W/M, daily till, Smart Cards, funnel + weekday heat + name lists in annex). Auto on the 1st; newest on the left.
bananoOmnipresent.reportEmpty: No archived reports yet. The first PDF is created on the next cron run (1st of the month), or contact support for a manual export if urgent.
bananoOmnipresent.voucherLoyaltyDesc: Same idea as the PDF above: auto-generated on the 1st (8:00 UTC) for the previous calendar month. CSV is downloadable; detail also under Customer base → Gift voucher archive.
bananoOmnipresent.voucherStaffDesc: Monthly export of € balance vouchers (same cron). See Customer base → Staff voucher archive for the live table.
bananoOmnipresent.noClientCategoryMonth: No customers in this category this month.
bananoOmnipresent.partialDataError: One dashboard section could not load (incomplete time data). Reload or check the console; if it persists, contact support.

bananoPilotageCore.day_subline_revenue_no_goal: Today’s revenue (amounts entered at checkout). Set a daily goal in your profile to enable the gauge.
bananoPilotageCore.day_insight_revenue_few: Few tickets with amounts today. Keep logging loyalty purchases to see your peak activity.
bananoPilotageCore.day_insight_revenue_golden_hour: Today, {share}% of your visits are between {hourStart}:00 and {hourEnd}:00 — your “golden hour” for staffing and restock.
bananoPilotageCore.day_subline_visit_no_goal: Volume logged at checkout (points / stamps). Enter the ticket total (incl. tax) to enable revenue reporting.
bananoPilotageCore.day_insight_visit_few: Not enough visits to pin down a reliable peak hour. Encourage logging every purchase.
bananoPilotageCore.day_insight_visit_peak: Today, {share}% of visits fall between {hourStart}:00 and {hourEnd}:00 — heaviest load in that window.
bananoPilotageCore.week_sub: Loyalty visits: this week vs last week (same Monday–Sunday range).
bananoPilotageCore.week_insight_low_data: Not enough data this week to compare days one by one. Keep logging visits.
bananoPilotageCore.week_insight_calm_day: {weekday} is your slowest day ({pctVsAvg} vs the weekly average visits). A targeted WhatsApp offer that day can smooth demand.
bananoPilotageCore.week_insight_saturday_append: Saturday note: about {share}% of visits in this period fall on a Saturday — plan restock and weekend staffing.
bananoPilotageCore.month_subline_avg_basket: {count, plural, one {Average ticket (tickets with amount) — # ticket this month.} other {Average ticket (tickets with amount) — # tickets this month.}}
bananoPilotageCore.month_insight_loyalty_uplift: Your regulars (3+ visits) average about {pct} higher tickets vs newer profiles this month — keep pushing sign-ups.
bananoPilotageCore.month_insight_increase_variety: Enter amounts on more varied profiles to compare occasional vs regular spenders.
bananoPilotageCore.month_subline_volume_no_amount: This month’s loyalty volume (no ticket amounts: average ticket not calculated).
bananoPilotageCore.month_insight_frequency_compare: Profiles with 3+ visits return {pct} more on average vs newer profiles this month — loyalty shows up in frequency.
bananoPilotageCore.month_insight_enter_amounts_hint: Once you enter incl.-tax amounts at checkout, average ticket and regular vs new gaps appear here.
bananoPilotageCore.month_subline_sparse_data: This month: still too little data.
bananoPilotageCore.month_insight_sparse: Log purchases and amounts to unlock the strategy view (average ticket, regular vs new gap).
bananoPilotageCore.card_champion_body_product: “{name}” leads: about {pct}% of your margin this period (catalog / checkout data).
bananoPilotageCore.card_champion_body_note: Most common purchase note this month: “{text}” ({count, plural, one {# time} other {# times}}). Connect a catalog for product / margin level.
bananoPilotageCore.card_champion_body_empty: No repeat purchase theme in checkout notes. Add a short detail (e.g. “BBQ bundle”) or connect your catalog.
bananoPilotageCore.card_stock_body_ratio: “{label}”: sales about {ratio}× vs forecast — check stock for the next few days.
bananoPilotageCore.card_stock_body_empty: Connect your item feed, wholesaler, or ERP for volume vs forecast alerts.

bananoCrmImport.subtitle: Excel, CSV or export from your previous till — column analysis followed by merging into REPUTEXA.
bananoCrmImport.drop_title: Drag and drop your file here
bananoCrmImport.pick_help: CSV/Excel export from your till or loyalty system: in Europe (GDPR), you can generally retrieve your customer file with one click. Phone numbers are converted to +44 (spaces, dashes, brackets removed; 07 and mobile numbers without 0 are managed). Without export, copy-pasting from the web page to Excel often works. Lines without a valid number are ignored. Multiple lines for the same number: the imported balance ({balanceWord}) is summed. Existing base: the file adds to the current balance. Current program mode: {programMode}.
bananoCrmImport.mapping_help: Compare the first three lines: indicate which column contains the phone number (labels may differ: Phone_Number, Telephone, etc.). Adjust other fields if auto-detection is not suitable.
bananoCrmImport.preview_footer: Preview of the first 5 lines (name = registered label: uppercase, without accents, apostrophes and punctuation → spaces). Detected columns: {detected}

bananoCrmImport.done_li_skipped: {n, plural, one {# line skipped (invalid phone)} other {# lines skipped (invalid phone)}}
bananoCrmImport.done_li_errors: {n, plural, one {# technical error — check the server console} other {# technical errors — check the server console}}

adminCouncilDigest.introRich: <brief>In brief:</brief> this log <notdebate>does not discuss</notdebate> between multiple AIs each time. These are <jobs>scheduled jobs</jobs> that copy the already existing state in the database (Sentinel, Guardian, Forge Babel, Nexus reminder). Nothing is executed on production without you.
adminCouncilDigest.cronHintRich: As long as the Vercel cron (<cronPath>/api/cron/admin-council-digest</cronPath> + secret <cronSecret>CRON_SECRET</cronSecret>) has not run, the list remains empty. However, you can force <firstLine>a first line</firstLine> right away:
adminCouncilDigest.prereqRich: Prerequisite: migration <migration>157_admin_council_digest</migration> applied and Supabase client service role configured.
adminCouncilDigest.dataIntroRich: Latest recorded lines. These are <photos>photos</photos> of the existing state, not a college of agents debating in a loop. Sensitive acts (legal email, production) remain with you.

adminCouncilDigestSeed.toastSuccess: New line added to the Council log.
adminCouncilDigestSeed.toastError: Unable to write to the log

securityPerfection.s1Rich: It’s the guard post of the fortress: a note, a log, and buttons to launch rounds or shut everything down if you have a doubt. The robots do not 'hack' anyone: they look cleanly.
securityPerfection.s2Rich: It’s the result of the last major audit, not a score that moves all day. If it’s low, read the list and make another pass after fixing something.
securityPerfection.s3Rich: You turn it on and all the automations in this area stop, like a wall switch. You turn it off whenever you want to resume the pace.
securityPerfection.s4Rich: More ideas and useful content during special missions, but no robot modifies your code, prices or site rendering without you.
securityPerfection.s5Rich: You see if the encrypted copy of your database has been sent to the cloud (variables like BACKUP_S3_*, AES key, database migration). Red or empty: you need to connect the right settings.
securityPerfection.s6Rich: This button opens the same room in pop-up. To settle down for a while, also open the page /dashboard/admin/security-perfection from the admin modules.

blackBox.s1Rich: Store old paperwork in compressed boxes on a cloud; the database keeps only the label for quick retrieval. Reviews remain visible to customers while you archive the heavy history.
blackBox.s2Rich: As long as there aren’t enough days of history (setting <mono>BLACK_BOX_HOT_RETENTION_DAYS</mono>), it’s normal for the attic to be empty. Without cloud passwords <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono>, the robot doesn’t leave.
blackBox.s3Li1Rich: Store now: like sweeping at night, but on demand.
blackBox.s3Li2Rich: Search: enter words or choose dates to find a box.
blackBox.s3Li3Rich: Open a preview: consult the content for proof or an audit.

iaForge.s1Rich: You see how they’re doing (scores), you take a snapshot of the curve with a snapshot, and you teach them the right tone by saying 'yes' or 'more like this' on their drafts. A large automatic clean-up can run on Sundays. No Git modifications from this screen.
iaForge.s2Rich: Always active, in bursts or large one-off analysis: you choose by assistant. The Sunday programme (/api/cron/ia-forge-batch) can launch a pass by itself if it’s connected.
iaForge.s3Rich: Take a snapshot after a big day or before a meeting: like noting the temperature on the fridge. The big 'analyse' buttons consume a bit of API — keep an eye on the bill.

nexus.s1Rich: The still open files are sorted from the most urgent to the calmest: red = quickly, orange = monitor, green = normal. A purple frame means 'the AI is unsure': reread before responding to the customer.
nexus.s2Rich: If the robot suggests regenerating a key or restarting a connection, you must click OK or No — once per suggestion, like a signature.
nexus.s3Rich: You write in two sentences what happened and how you fixed it: the next time a similar case arises, the machine will remember. Never put real passwords in the visible customer chat.

codeGuardian.s1Rich: We browse the code like a technical check: tasks to clean up, possible slowdowns, large wobbly pieces. Nothing is pushed automatically to Git; you copy the list or send it to your team.
codeGuardian.s2Rich: It’s the last major complete round, not a live measure of the site in production. A score that drops after a big delivery isn’t necessarily a disaster: mainly check if it rises over time.
codeGuardian.s3Rich: Paste a piece of code and two lines of context: you receive an opinion without execution, just to read and decide.

legalPublish.s1Rich: Which document (site rules, privacy, notices), the full text, a summary for humans (mandatory for emails), and when it starts to apply — with the legal delay if it’s not immediate. The form blocks absurd dates.
legalPublish.s2Rich: You can have it written, translated or send a test email. If you import a Guardian draft, have it reviewed by a competent human before pressing Publish.
legalPublish.s3Rich: Accounts receive a reminder; on the scheduled date, a small window asks people to confirm they understood until they validate. The detailed buttons are explained above the form.

auditKit.s1Rich: Here you show evidence, upload clean files, and check a list for an auditor or investor — in addition to the daily Guardian summary, not instead.
auditKit.s2Rich: The link Complete Guide — verification and maintenance opens the long document: technical inventory (migrations, APIs, legal_versioning, consent_logs), frequency matrices, checklists (section 8), operational calendar (section 12), etc. This page reminds you of them with habits to check and an operator sheet; the guide remains the detailed reference for everything the screen cannot copy.
auditKit.s3Rich: Before an audit visit; crossing it with what the cookie banners and the legal texts already published on the site say.

adminBabelGuardian.subtitle: Local Overlord · smart-merge · checklist
adminBabelGuardian.overlordP1: Last step of the assistant: disk writing, merging snippets into .ts, email stubs, optional git commit. Requires
adminBabelGuardian.philosophyP2: . For a language to be truly 'native' (no word out of locale), follow steps 1 → 12 below in order, then test. The assistant
adminBabelGuardian.demoP1: Review placeholders in demo live in
adminBabelGuardian.demoP3: (to run if you use it in your flow).

adminIaForgeClient.errStateHint: Check Supabase migration 141_ia_forge and try again.
adminIaForgeClient.errStateHintRich: Check Supabase <mono>141_ia_forge</mono> migration and try again.
adminIaForgeClient.guideWhatBodyRich: You see if the assistants are doing well (scores and curves), you take snapshot photos over time, and you teach them the style by saying 'yes' or 'more like this' on their drafts. The 'big clean-up' button enriches useful records for negotiations and support — without touching the Git repository from here.
adminIaForgeClient.guideButtonsLi1Rich: Refresh — Re-reads the figures without launching a paid analysis.
adminIaForgeClient.guideButtonsLi2Rich: Photo of scores — Adds a point on the curve (after a big day or before a meeting).
adminIaForgeClient.guideButtonsLi3Rich: Big AI clean-up — Gives a smart sweep (costs a bit of API): new records and reusable ideas.
adminIaForgeClient.guideSpeedsBodyRich: Always awake for the one you choose, in bursts for a faster pace (often on Sunday with /api/cron/ia-forge-batch), or large analysis when you paste important text at the bottom of the page. Only one mode active at a time per assistant on this screen.
adminIaForgeClient.guideRlhfLi1Rich: Feed — Adds examples to review (often very red reviews).
adminIaForgeClient.guideRlhfLi2Rich: Validate — 'Yes, like this is good', to reinforce the right style.
adminIaForgeClient.guideRlhfLi3Rich: Correct — You rewrite the correct sentence when the robot missed it; it remembers better next time.
adminIaForgeClient.guideEmptyChartsBodyRich: Take a few score photos or wait for the Sunday pass. Optional settings FORGE_* in .env.example refine the behaviour.
adminIaForgeClient.chartSectionTitle: Curves of 'are they responding well' (score photos)
adminIaForgeClient.chartEmpty: Not enough points yet to draw a curve. Click on 'Score photo' a few times or wait for the Sunday pass (<mono>/api/cron/ia-forge-batch</mono>).
adminIaForgeClient.agentsSectionTitle: Notes by assistant & setting the pace
adminIaForgeClient.modeContinuousLabel: Always awake — the robot remains in active learning
adminIaForgeClient.modeBurstLabel: In bursts — often linked to the big clean-up on Sunday evening
adminIaForgeClient.modeDeepLabel: Large analysis all at once — when you paste important text
adminIaForgeClient.toastAnalyzeOk: Analysis {depth}: {snippets} excerpts saved, {cross} cross-ideas for learning
adminIaForgeClient.toastRlhfSeed: {count} decision(s) added to the human feedback queue
adminIaForgeClient.rlhfIntro: 'Yes' reinforces the right tone; 'No + your sentence' teaches the robot to do better next time.
adminIaForgeClient.rlhfEmpty: Nothing pending. Click 'Fill...' or wait for a case to arrive by itself.
adminIaForgeClient.deepSectionHint: E.g. emails from a competitor, list of objections: we compare it to recent reviews and produce reusable records for tone and negotiation.
adminIaForgeClient.crossLearnEmpty: Nothing for now. Launch a 'Big AI clean-up' or the deep analysis to extract useful objections from support and reviews.
adminIaForgeClient.snippetsEmpty: No excerpts for now — launch an analysis.

adminGrowthWarRoom.localesP3: ). Missing keys in a locale fall back to the
adminGrowthWarRoom.localesP5: Supabase, aligned with the establishment when connected) drives auth / onboarding emails.
adminGrowthWarRoom.localesP6: Add a language (e.g. Korean): edit the catalogue + create
adminGrowthWarRoom.localesP7: , then deploy. No magic button: the build must know the locale. Run the scripts
adminGrowthWarRoom.syncAllCronNote: Same logic as the cron /api/cron/outreach-active-countries (Bearer CRON_SECRET), without manual cap per request: each country uses its
adminGrowthWarRoom.syncAllCronNoteRich: Same logic as the cron <mono>/api/cron/outreach-active-countries</mono> (Bearer <mono2>CRON_SECRET</mono2>), without manual cap per request: each country uses its <mono3>dailyOutreachCap</mono3>.
adminGrowthWarRoom.pushInstantlyBody: Requires INSTANTLY_API_KEY, provider 'instantly' and a campaign ID. Leads become CONTACTED only if the API responds OK.
adminGrowthWarRoom.pushInstantlyBodyRich: Requires <mono>INSTANTLY_API_KEY</mono>, provider 'instantly' and a campaign ID. Leads become CONTACTED only if the API responds OK.
adminGrowthWarRoom.legalNoteP2: continues to manage the legal publications of the product (T&Cs, privacy) from the admin home. For a given country, validate the texts in the target language; this panel does not yet automatically generate local law for cold-email campaigns.
adminGrowthWarRoom.localesPara1Rich: The public interface and server emails that pass through messages/*.json use the same list as Next.js (lib/i18n/site-locales-catalog.ts). Missing keys in a locale fall back to French. The account language (profiles.language Supabase, aligned with the establishment when connected) drives auth / onboarding emails.
adminGrowthWarRoom.localesPara2Rich: <warn>Add a language (e.g. Korean): edit the catalogue + create <mono>messages/ko.json</mono>, then deploy. No magic button: the build must know the locale. Run the scripts <mono2>merge-*</mono2> in <mono3>scripts/</mono3> to merge patches (auth, legal, etc.).</warn>
adminGrowthWarRoom.publishLocaleCheckbox: Publish the locale on the site (e.g. /it, /es) — otherwise 404 for visitors
adminGrowthWarRoom.noDomains: No domain — add the hostname (without http).
adminGrowthWarRoom.missionValidationHelp: The product must be chosen from the validated list (targets/settings.json). An entry is logged in outreach with server control.
adminGrowthWarRoom.missionValidationMissing: Please provide the prospect ID and product.

bananoEliteClients.pageIntro: Your top 5 customers for the month (sum of ticket amounts entered at checkout + visits). Write a personalized offer — nothing is sent without your confirmation.
bananoEliteClients.emptyHint: Amounts come from tickets recorded at the loyalty terminal. The more your team enters totals, the more accurate the ranking.
bananoEliteClients.offerPlaceholder: E.g. 20% off your favorite pastry this week, or a free coffee on your next visit…
bananoEliteClients.offerHint: The WhatsApp message will automatically include your business name and a warm tone, in your REPUTEXA account language.
bananoEliteClients.disclaimer: No automatic blasts: you trigger each message. Respect marketing consent and opt-out (STOP) — same as other WhatsApp sends.
bananoEliteClients.toastSendError: Could not send. Check Twilio or try again.
bananoEliteClients.phoneMissing: No phone on file — add it in the customer database.
bananoEliteClients.confirmBody: The customer will receive a WhatsApp message on their phone. This is final (same as a manual send).
bananoEliteClients.suggestOfferBody: Petite attention sur {favorite} pour vous remercier de votre fidélité ce mois-ci (détail à préciser avec votre équipe).
bananoEliteClients.sharedBaseIntro: Rédigez une seule fois la règle commerciale (pourcentage, produit, dates). Vous pouvez la copier sur chaque ligne puis lancer la personnalisation IA : chaque client reçoit la même offre, formulée différemment.
bananoEliteClients.sharedBaseLabel: Texte de l’offre (identique pour tout le monde avant personnalisation)
bananoEliteClients.sharedBasePlaceholder: Ex. : -15 % sur toute la carte du vendredi 25 au dimanche 27 avril, hors menus enfants.
bananoEliteClients.applyBaseToAllRows: Mettre cette base sur les 5 lignes
bananoEliteClients.polishAllAi: Personnaliser les 5 messages avec l’IA (même promo, textes différents)
bananoEliteClients.polishAllNeedBase: Rédigez d’abord la base commune, ou au moins une ligne d’offre.
bananoEliteClients.polishAllDone: Personnalisation terminée pour le classement affiché.
bananoEliteClients.vocalForAllHint: One recording can go with every message. If the promo base is empty, your voice is transcribed into the offer text automatically. You can also record per row below.
bananoEliteClients.speechRecognitionDenied: Allow the browser’s speech recognition for live dictation.
bananoEliteClients.transcribeFilledOfferBase: Offer text filled from your voice recording.
bananoEliteClients.transcribeSkippedBaseFilled: Existing offer text was kept. Use “Transcribe voice into base” to replace it with the transcript.
bananoEliteClients.transcribeReplaceConfirm: Replace all offer text with the voice transcript?
bananoEliteClients.vocalAttachedHint: Vocal joint en plus du texte (WhatsApp).
bananoEliteClients.recordingNotSupported: Micro ou enregistrement non disponible sur ce navigateur.
bananoEliteClients.bulkIntro: Rédigez un texte commun, cochez les destinataires avec numéro, puis envoyez. Le vocal « pour tous » s'applique aussi ici.
bananoEliteClients.bulkNeedSelection: Cochez au moins un client avec numéro.
bananoEliteClients.bulkNeedText: Rédigez une offre pour l'envoi groupé.
bananoEliteClients.archiveIntro: Historique de ce qui a été envoyé : client, mois de référence, extrait de l’offre, vocal joint, identifiant WhatsApp.
bananoEliteClients.offerRowHint: Prérempli depuis la base commune ou personnalisé par l’IA. Vous pouvez encore éditer avant envoi.

whatsappReviewMeta.title: WhatsApp Reviews — REPUTEXA
whatsappReviewMeta.description: Zenith space: WhatsApp reviews, Wallet pass loyalty with a public QR, management, and customer database.
whatsappReviewMeta.pageSubtitleEcommerce: Online store: Zenith collection and webhook; customer loyalty via public Wallet link (QR).
whatsappReviewMeta.pageSubtitleFull: Zenith collection, Wallet loyalty (display your QR: customers scan and add Apple Wallet or Google Wallet depending on their phone, with autofill when the browser allows), management, customer database, and settings.
whatsappReviewMeta.clientsBoundaryDescription: Reload the page. If the problem persists, open the console (F12) for technical details.
whatsappReviewMeta.pilotageVipFooterCompact: VIP of the month: open Settings - WhatsApp follow-ups (typical auto-send on the 1st, Paris timezone).
whatsappReviewMeta.pilotageVipArticleTitle: Reward your best customer of the period: a personalised message strengthens the relationship.
whatsappReviewMeta.pilotageVipRankingHint: Ranking by total ticket amounts entered over the period (cumulative, not a single purchase).
whatsappReviewMeta.pilotageVipFooterC: . Automatic sending typically on the 1st of the month (previous calendar month, Paris timezone).
whatsappReviewMeta.terminalSeoDescription: Tablet mode: loyalty checkout, full-screen interface. Add to home screen from the browser.

whatsappReviewError.title: WhatsApp Reviews — Error
whatsappReviewError.fallbackMessage: Unexpected error. Please try again or reload the page.

adminBlackBoxArchive.skippedNoEligible: Nothing is old enough: only entries older than {days} days go to the archive.
adminBlackBoxArchive.skippedNoBackupS3: Cloud storage not configured on the server (check BACKUP_S3_* and redeploy if you just added them).
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideWhatForBody: We store <hl>old traces</hl> (support, files, messages…) in a well-compressed cloud vault. In the database, there remains mainly a <hl>small index</hl> to search and prove what happened — not all the raw data in one place.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideBeforeLi1: The 'Black Box' database migration must have been run (the one that creates the index and history tables).
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideBeforeLi2: The cloud storage identifiers (<mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono>, same family as Sentinel Vault) must be on <hl>the host running the API</hl>, not just on your PC.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideBeforeLi3: Archiving must remain enabled (<mono>BLACK_BOX_ARCHIVE_ENABLED</mono> not set to <mono>0</mono>).
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideWhyNothingBody: Setting <mono>BLACK_BOX_HOT_RETENTION_DAYS</mono> (often 30 days, minimum 7): we only touch entries <hl>older</hl> than this timeframe. Everything fresh → zero batch, this is intended. The grey banner at the top reminds the number of days.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideTablesLi1: Support logs, completed files, contact messages: after a successful archiving, the 'hot' copy can be deleted; the proof remains in the vault.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideTablesLi2: Public reviews: we rather make a <hl>double</hl> side archive, without immediately deleting the original; a marker avoids copying the same thing twice.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideButtonsLi1: <hl>Refresh</hl>: reload the index and the latest runs.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideButtonsLi2: <hl>Start Archiving</hl>: same work as an auto run at night, with counters (batches, rows, bytes) returned.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideButtonsLi3: <hl>Search</hl>: keyword in indexed text, dates intersecting the batch period, filter by table if needed.
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideButtonsLi4: <hl>Decompress & Preview</hl>: the server will fetch the file from the cloud and display the content (read, proof).
adminBlackBoxArchive.guideAutomationBody: A scheduled task can call <mono>GET|POST /api/cron/black-box-archive</mono> with the intended secret (e.g. Vercel). You can enable short AI summaries per batch with <mono>BLACK_BOX_AI_SUMMARY=1</mono> + OpenAI key. The duration class on the S3 side is set with <mono>BLACK_BOX_S3_STORAGE_CLASS</mono>.
adminBlackBoxArchive.statsLastRunLine: Last run: {status} · {date} · {rows, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
adminBlackBoxArchive.metaHotIntro: <hl>Hot data:</hl> we only archive what is older than <days></days> days (minimum 7 on the server). If everything is brand new → no batch, this is normal.
adminBlackBoxArchive.metaS3Help: Required on the <hl>same server</hl> as the API (e.g. Vercel → Env + redeployment): <mono>BACKUP_S3_BUCKET</mono> or <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_S3_BUCKET</mono>, + <mono>BACKUP_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID</mono> or <mono>BACKUP_S3_KEY</mono>, + <mono>BACKUP_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY</mono> (alias <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_S3_*</mono> accepted). R2: <mono>BACKUP_S3_ENDPOINT</mono> + <mono>BACKUP_S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE=1</mono>. A <mono>.env.local</mono> is not sufficient in production if not injected on the host.
adminBlackBoxArchive.limitSelectTitle: Maximum number of batches returned (server caps at 80)
adminBlackBoxArchive.filtersHint: Filters applied automatically after input (debounce ~320 ms). Refresh button = reload everything.
adminBlackBoxArchive.emptyHotExplanation: No batch in database. Most common cause: all your rows are still 'hot' (more recent than ) — archiving and cron do not create any index.
adminBlackBoxArchive.emptyHintSecondary: Otherwise: migration <mono>142</mono>, variables <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono> on the host (Vercel), 'Start Archiving' button or cron <mono>/api/cron/black-box-archive</mono> with secret.
adminBlackBoxArchive.emptyS3Warning: The server does not see a complete S3 config: check <mono>BACKUP_S3_BUCKET</mono> + keys (+ possibly R2 endpoint).

adminBabelPlaybook.compactP1: A truly native language = all steps marked 'For a native locale' on the main Babel Guardian page, plus human proofreading. The AI assistant below only speeds up one file (messages/xx.json): this is neither mandatory nor sufficient by itself.
adminBabelPlaybook.panelP1Prefix: Prefer a screen-by-screen flow with AI previews and backup?
adminBabelPlaybook.bullet1: Nothing is imposed. You can ignore Expansion (AI) and do everything manually, or use it solely to save time on the JSON.
adminBabelPlaybook.bullet2: 'Native' = consistency everywhere. If a step is missing, the user can still see French or English (next-intl fallback, emails, SEO, signup).
adminBabelPlaybook.bullet3: Recommended order: catalogue → messages → email pack → SEO → signup → War Room → deployment → manual check.
adminBabelPlaybook.catalogGateLine: Public /{code} if country {country} is enabled (War Room)
adminBabelPlaybook.newLangTitle: Add a new language (e.g. nl) — step by step
adminBabelPlaybook.newLangIntro: Replace xx / nl with your ISO code in the paths below.
adminBabelPlaybook.step01_title: Declare the locale in the URL catalogue
adminBabelPlaybook.step01_p1: File <mono>lib/i18n/site-locales-catalog.ts</mono>: add <mono>nl</mono> in <mono>SITE_LOCALE_CODES</mono> and a line in <mono>SITE_LOCALE_META</mono> (admin label + possibly <mono>gateCountryCode</mono> if the public site <mono>/nl</mono> should wait for the War Room).
adminBabelPlaybook.step01_p2: Without this: no route <mono>/nl/…</mono>, middleware and emails do not recognise the language.
adminBabelPlaybook.step02_p1: Create <mono>messages/nl.json</mono> with the same structure as <mono>messages/fr.json</mono>. To avoid any visible French words, aim for complete key coverage (the runtime still merges with French for missing keys).
adminBabelPlaybook.step02_p2b: → AI draft to proofread, then copy the validated JSON into the repository under <mono>messages/nl.json</mono>.
adminBabelPlaybook.step03_p2: Without this: emails passing through this pack fall back to default behaviour (often French).
adminBabelPlaybook.step04_p1: Check <mono>lib/emails/auth-email-i18n.ts</mono>: the allowed locales follow the catalogue; if a specific rule exists for your code, align it.
adminBabelPlaybook.step05_p1: File <mono>lib/i18n/signup-ui-by-locale.ts</mono>: add the equivalent block for <mono>nl</mono> (transcreation, not word-for-word translation of FR examples).
adminBabelPlaybook.step06_p1: File <mono>app/[locale]/layout.tsx</mono>: add <mono>nl</mono> in <mono>SEO_CONFIG</mono> (metadata in the target language).
adminBabelPlaybook.step07_p1: <mono>i18n/routing.ts</mono> already imports <mono>SITE_LOCALE_CODES</mono>: no modification if step 1 is done. <mono>generateStaticParams</mono> in the locale layout follows.
adminBabelPlaybook.step08_p1: <mono>lib/i18n/negotiate-site-locale.ts</mono> and the middleware use the catalogue: once <mono>nl</mono> is declared, <mono>Accept-Language</mono> can direct to <mono>/nl</mono> (according to existing rules).
adminBabelPlaybook.step09_p1a: If gateCountryCode is filled in step 1, open the
adminBabelPlaybook.step09_p1b: and enable the country + 'public site' for <mono>/xx</mono> to be served.
adminBabelPlaybook.step10_p1: Audit the sends in <mono>lib/emails/templates.ts</mono>, <mono>lib/emails/info-broadcast-email.ts</mono> and the associated API routes: hardcoded texts or limited languages must include <mono>nl</mono> (or <mono>profiles.language</mono>) to avoid mixing languages.
adminBabelPlaybook.step11_p1: If you maintain pieces by language (<mono>scripts/merge-auth-flow-locales.mjs</mono>, etc.), rerun them after adding source files. Otherwise, ignore this step.
adminBabelPlaybook.step12_p1: Deploy to regenerate routes. Then browse: home <mono>/nl</mono>, signup, test email (magic link / reset), a dashboard screen with example texts, and any legal / cookie flow if concerned.
adminBabelPlaybook.footerP1: As long as a key is missing in <mono>messages/nl.json</mono>, the interface may display the French value. As long as an email template is not connected to the locale, the user may receive another language. The checklist above aims to close these gaps one by one, at your own pace; AI does not replace this verification.

adminSecurityPerfection.toastAutoIntensive: Intensive mode: checks approximately every 15 minutes (Vercel cron).
adminSecurityPerfection.toastAutoDaily: Daily at random UTC slot enabled — next draw displayed below.
adminSecurityPerfection.toastShieldOn: Auto shield: URL patterns merged after useful rounds (middleware + legal config, not the repository).
adminSecurityPerfection.toastShieldOff: Auto shield disabled: rounds no longer enrich these patterns.
adminSecurityPerfection.vaultFailedBodyDefault: Check your email or Telegram alerts. Often: a missing cloud password, a wrong key, or the scheduled robot that did not run.
adminSecurityPerfection.vaultRunningTitle: A backup may have been in progress
adminSecurityPerfection.vaultRunningBody: If it remains stuck for a long time, check that the nightly cron is running properly and that Vercel has enough time to finish the task.
adminSecurityPerfection.vaultStaleTitle: It's been a while since a successful copy
adminSecurityPerfection.vaultStaleBody: Last successful run: {date} UTC. Check the robot's schedule (around 3 AM UTC) and your settings.
adminSecurityPerfection.pageSubtitleRich: We perform a <hl>security check</hl> without breaking the castle: the robots check the doors, they do not steal client secrets. The big red button stops everything before the super-help mode.
adminSecurityPerfection.overlayNote: Score out of 100, log of checks, emergency button, and 'super-help' mode without dangerous magic (no one modifies your code or prices on their own).
adminSecurityPerfection.guideWhatBodyRich: A <hl>control room</hl> to see if your digital fortress is doing well: a score, a list of things spotted, and buttons to launch checks or stop everything if you panic a bit. Nothing goes without you clicking (except the little robots you allow with the settings).
adminSecurityPerfection.guideScoreBodyRich: Like a <hl>test after the last check</hl>, not like a thermometer that changes every second. If the score is low, check the list below and run another check after fixing something.
adminSecurityPerfection.guideKillBodyRich: The <hl>big stop</hl>: it turns off automatic checks and super-help mode, like cutting the power to a toy that makes too much noise. You turn it back on when you feel ready.
adminSecurityPerfection.guideGodBodyRich: The mode '<hl>AI gives you more ideas</hl>' during special missions, but <hl>it does not touch your code, prices, or site design on its own</hl>.
adminSecurityPerfection.guideVaultBodyRich: It's the <hl>encrypted backup copy</hl> of your database, stored in a cloud vault (requires <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono> and <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_AES_KEY</mono>, and the Vault migration). If nothing appears, the robot has not yet succeeded in making the copy.
adminSecurityPerfection.guideRobotTitle: Robot that runs on its own
adminSecurityPerfection.guideRobotBodyRich: You choose <hl>how often</hl> a little robot makes a round of guard. As long as the stop is on, it does nothing — even if the alarm goes off in the background.
adminSecurityPerfection.killBannerBody: Everything is paused on the server side. When it's calm, you can turn it back on.
adminSecurityPerfection.killHelpPastille: Like turning off the TV when it gets too loud: everything that runs on its own here stops. You can turn it back on whenever you want.
adminSecurityPerfection.killCardDesc: You press, and hop: no more auto checks, no more super-help mode, until you reopen it.
adminSecurityPerfection.killTitleOn: You plug it back in: the robots can start working again.
adminSecurityPerfection.killTitleOff: You unplug it: this entire module takes a break immediately, without breaking files.
adminSecurityPerfection.godHelpPastille: The AI talks to you more and offers ideas during special missions, but it does not modify your site, prices, or appearance on its own.
adminSecurityPerfection.godCardDescRich: When it's on, you get <hl>extra advice</hl> for 'ghost' missions — without the robot rewriting your files, prices, or design for you. The <hl>URL shield</hl> (in the 'Robot' frame) is separate: patterns for the middleware are merged into the legal config, not into the Git repository.
adminSecurityPerfection.godTitleKillFirst: First turn off the emergency stop, then you can turn this back on.
adminSecurityPerfection.godTitleGodOff: You turn off the enhanced advice mode for the next steps.
adminSecurityPerfection.godTitleGodOn: You turn on the 'more ideas' mode, without dangerous automatic delegation.
adminSecurityPerfection.robotSectionTitle: Robot that runs on its own
adminSecurityPerfection.robotIntroRich: <hl>Ghost Protocol</hl> is the guardian that patrols the castle on a <hl2>calendar</hl2> you choose: it looks at a different thing each time, without breaking the walls. Then it writes what it saw in the log and updates your score. It does not drop files on its own in your project. This is <hl3>not a 'zero pirate' promise</hl3>: limited probes (throttling, no destructive load), not a red team across the surface — the optional shield below does not replace WAF, review, secrets, or external audit.
adminSecurityPerfection.scheduleOffHelp: The robot does nothing on its own; you stay at the buttons.
adminSecurityPerfection.scheduleHourlyHelp: Basically a little round every hour, if the server allows.
adminSecurityPerfection.scheduleDailyHelp: Once a day at a randomly drawn hour, to avoid always loading at the same time.
adminSecurityPerfection.scheduleIntensiveHelp: The robot can pass often (approximately every 15 minutes), as long as the stop is not on.
adminSecurityPerfection.shieldToggleHelp: Conservative patterns only on the query; stored in legal_config — no Git writing.
adminSecurityPerfection.shieldDesc: When a round raises signals on certain pillars, safe regex patterns can be added to refuse obvious loads in the URL. Targeted control — not unlimited pentesting, not a replacement for a managed WAF or human correction in the source code.
adminSecurityPerfection.cronNoteRich: The server alarm goes off approximately <hl>every 15 minutes</hl>: but this is only if you have allowed a rhythm above that the little robot actually wakes up.
adminSecurityPerfection.sentinelHelpPastille: Secret copy of your entire database in a cloud vault. Requires the database migration + cloud passwords in Vercel; the robot works at night.
adminSecurityPerfection.sentinelIntroRich: Imagine a <hl>photo of your entire filing cabinet</hl>, compressed, put in a sealed envelope, then sent to a vault keeper (S3). The passwords are <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono> and <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_AES_KEY</mono>. The scheduled robot (Vercel cron + access secret) makes the journey without loading everything at once into memory.
adminSecurityPerfection.sentinelNoTrace: No trace yet: run the Vault migration on the database and set the cloud variables on Vercel for the first copy to go.
adminSecurityPerfection.ipNoteRich: <lead>If you move or change your router:</lead> your <hl>internet address</hl> often changes. Update the <mono>ADMIN_ALLOWED_IPS</mono> list on Vercel like a new <hl>access card</hl>, and check your details in the settings — otherwise, the door may refuse without warning quickly.
adminSecurityPerfection.ghostBtnHelp: You manually launch a 'ghost' round: a step of the castle tour, then the summary goes into the log and the score.
adminSecurityPerfection.scanBtnHelp: You request a major check immediately: like opening all the cupboards at once, but without breaking the dishes — the score and list update.
adminSecurityPerfection.filterEmpty: Nothing at this severity level — widen the filter.
adminSecurityPerfection.footerRich: Here it is mainly <hl>security and backup</hl>. To know if your site 'feels fast' for real humans, you will use other tables (real user measures), not just this room.

adminNexusSupport.title: Nexus Live
adminNexusSupport.helpPastille: The support queue: who is most urgent, what the AI believes it has understood, and what it intends to do — but it awaits your approval for actual actions.
adminNexusSupport.subtitleRich: Floating window: click on the <blur>blurred background</blur>, the cross, or Esc to return to the admin. Coloured borders = who takes precedence.
adminNexusSupport.guideTwoRich: You see the open cases, from most urgent to least, what the AI thinks it knows (facts / doubts), and you must say yes or no before it touches a key or connection. At the end, you write the lesson for similar cases in the future.
adminNexusSupport.guideColorViolet: Purple border — The AI is uncertain: review before responding to the customer.
adminNexusSupport.guideOpen: Customer name, bar 'how urgent it is', badge 'how sure the AI is'. The 'Customer view' link opens their space. 'Close' asks for a brief summary before filing the case away.
adminNexusSupport.guideApproveRich: Each technical idea (webhook, new key…) has its internal ticket. Approve = we do it once. Reject = we forget it. No passwords in the visible customer chat.
adminNexusSupport.guideAfter: Your brief verdict feeds the memory for future cases of the same kind. Refresh the list if someone else has made changes in parallel.
adminNexusSupport.autoPoll: Auto ~45s (tab visible) · {count, plural, one {# open} other {# open}}
adminNexusSupport.emptyFilter: No cases match the filter — broaden your search or revert to 'All'.
adminNexusSupport.selectPromptRich: Select a case from the queue on the left. The <red>red</red> cards are urgent severity; the <violet>pulsing purple</violet> outline indicates a diagnostic confidence < 80 (review).
adminNexusSupport.closeVerdictTitle: You note the verdict manually: it is stored for response assistance, then the case is filed away.
adminNexusSupport.aiSuggestHelp: Nothing goes out on its own: everything awaits your click. Approving triggers the action once on the admin server; rejecting discards the proposal.
adminNexusSupport.diagnosticHelp: Facts = actual readings at bootstrap. Doubts = explicit limitations. This is not a promise of future capabilities: it is the audit snapshot.

adminClientsSection.title: Clients
adminClientsSection.realtimeTitle: Automatic invalidation on profile change + infinite scrolling
adminClientsSection.guideSearchRich: Search: server-side filter on premises, name, email, phone; the list reloads with cursor pagination.
adminClientsSection.guideFiltersRich: Filters Critical / Watch / Healthy: segments by priority score (server-calculated) to frame interventions.
adminClientsSection.guideRealtimeRich: Real-time: cache invalidation when profiles change; infinite scrolling to load more.
adminClientsSection.guideSheetRich: Click a line or the Sheet button (fixed to the right) for details (usage, subscription, actions).
adminClientsSection.activeSearchBanner: Active search — the 'Registered Users' cards at the top of the page count all profiles.
adminClientsSection.summaryNoSearch: {loaded, plural, one {# premise displayed} other {# premises displayed}} — {total, plural, one {# account in total} other {# accounts in total}}
adminClientsSection.summaryWithSearch: {loaded, plural, one {# line displayed} other {# lines displayed}} · {total, plural, one {# customer matches} other {# customers match}} the filter · search '{q}'

adminOpsStatusBar.mod_security_blurb: Security score, control log, emergency stop, super-help mode, vault copy.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_compliance_blurb: Compliance monitoring, drafts, queue before publication of legal texts.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_blackBox_blurb: Archive store, base labels, search, night robot.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_iaForge_blurb: Curves, human validation of responses, note photos, grand AI tidying.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_nexus_blurb: Support queue, emergencies, diagnostics, your OK before action, lesson at the end.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_codeGuardian_blurb: Code work list, read-only, test bench, zero auto push.
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_sentinel_blurb: Details of indicators, incidents, repair tracks (bottom of admin page).
adminOpsStatusBar.mod_legalPublish_blurb: Publish site texts + email reminders to accounts.
adminOpsStatusBar.hintLine: Tab only when visible · poll ~30s · hovering over a card = live detail

adminClientDetail.identityFootnote: Raw profile detail, Auth and premises below. First/last names are derived from the 'full_name' field when it contains multiple words.
adminClientDetail.legalVersionAtProof: Version at the time of proof
adminClientDetail.aiBudgetBody: Counter of billable LLM calls (consultant, sentiment stats, weekly insight). Beyond the hard cap: HTTP 429. Beyond the soft cap: slight throttling (pause). Defaults via env <soft>AI_LLM_DAILY_SOFT_DEFAULT</soft> / <hard>AI_LLM_DAILY_HARD_DEFAULT</hard>.
adminClientDetail.aiBudgetNone: No line yet — will be created at the first LLM call.
adminClientDetail.gdprBody: Deletes the Auth Supabase account (cascades on most business tables). Attempts to cancel Stripe if a subscription is linked. Third-party logs (Resend, etc.) are not automatically erasable: handle them according to your procedures.
adminClientDetail.toastBudgetInvalid: Invalid caps: hard must be strictly greater than soft (≥ 1).

legalPublish.aiHintParagraph: The AI runs on your server (Vercel) with the project's API keys — not via Cursor. Draft structured by document type; set LEGAL_PUBLISHER_* variables on the host to pre-fill the editor. Review, complete the [To complete], legal validation mandatory before publication.
legalPublish.consentPreview_footer: By continuing to use REPUTEXA, you accept the new terms.
legalPublish.guideBullet1: Choose the <docType>type of document</docType> (T&Cs, privacy, legal notice), then write or paste the content and a <chg>summary of changes</chg> (mandatory for communication and previews).
legalPublish.guideBullet2: The <eff>effective date</eff> is checked (minimum notice, cap); the <utc>time UTC</utc> specifies the moment for PENDING / ACTIVE, emails, and the modal.
legalPublish.guideBullet3: AI buttons: <ai>generation / translation / email / Sentinel review</ai> as available; open the modals <mod>document preview, email, consent</mod> before publishing if your process requires it.
legalPublish.guideBullet4: A <gd>Guardian draft</gd> can be pre-filled: import it then legally verify before 'Publish'.
legalPublish.guideBullet5: <pub>Publish</pub> saves the version and notifies according to the config; the browser draft is cleared after success.
legalPublish.draftAutoSaveHint: Draft: auto-save in this browser (refresh, return later).
legalPublish.guardianVerified: Verified on {verifiedAt} (UTC) — you can import then finalise the publication.
legalPublish.guardianComplianceHint: Go to <c>Compliance</c> and click 'I certify I have reviewed sources + draft' before publishing.
legalPublish.serverAiMissing: Server: no API key — configure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY
legalPublish.placeholderNotesIa: E.g. Add a clause on AI usage, specify retention periods of 3 years, update DPO address...
legalPublish.placeholderContentHtml: Paste or generate the HTML content of the document here...
legalPublish.labelSummaryHint: — displayed in the email & consent modal
legalPublish.placeholderSummary: E.g. Addition of clauses related to AI usage, update of personal data processing timelines...
legalPublish.introEffectiveDate: The <d>date</d> follows legal notice rules; the <h>time</h> is in <u>UTC</u> (also displayed in emails and user modal).
legalPublish.helpUtcRule: Rule (UTC): if you choose a date strictly after today, it must be at least {earliest} — that is {minDays} calendar days after {today}. E.g. if 'today' = 30 March: prohibited on 31 March → 28 April (less than 30 days); OK from 29 April; also OK 'in 72 days' or later (up to {maxDate}). Only {today} remains possible for immediate effect (excluding scheduling). Upon publication, an email announces the summary and effective date.
legalPublish.helpPendingModal: PENDING status until the day: the acceptance modal on the dashboard only appears from the effective date, once per version; after acceptance, the merchant is aligned with this version in Supabase (visible on the admin side).
legalPublish.sentinel_emptyHint: Compare to the published text if it exists; for a <fv>first version</fv>, an <rv>AI background review</rv> is executed on the server (questions / answers, GDPR, placeholders). Sensitive topics on personal data are flagged separately.
legalPublish.sentinel_rgpdFallback: Changes potentially impacting GDPR compliance have been detected. Check with your DPO before publication.
legalPublish.sentinel_fixIntro: Indicate the <d>actual data</d> (production URL, company name, SIREN/SIRET, headquarters, emails, DPO, named subcontractors, cookies link…). The AI rewrites the <h>HTML content</h> of the form by injecting them. If a Sentinel analysis is displayed above, it is transmitted as a checklist. Then, click 'Analyse' again to validate.

adminBroadcastEmailForm.introRich: Information message (not a legal document). You are composing in English; upon global dispatch, the server <auto>automatically generates</auto> versions in fr, es, de, it from the same content; each user receives the email in their <profilesCode>profiles.locale</profilesCode>. The preview is sent to <adminEmail>ADMIN_EMAIL</adminEmail> as a French client would see it; then the dispatch to all is unlocked (~1 hour on this exact version). The <schedWord>schedule</schedWord> (date + time below) does not impose <negation>any</negation> 30-day notice for legal publications — minimum window ~30 seconds, maximum ~12 months.
adminBroadcastEmailForm.scheduleHelpRich: Timezone: <browser>that of your browser</browser> (converted to UTC for the server). No preview token required; the message will be sent at the scheduled time if the migration and <cron>CRON_SECRET</cron> / Vercel cron are in place.

adminGrandCentralGuide.s1Title: The coloured bar at the top
adminGrandCentralGuide.s1Li1Rich: <green>Green</green>: the base and connected services are responding, the last vault copy succeeded, no major blocking compliance alerts.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s1Li2Rich: <orange>Orange</orange>: an issue is lingering (slowness, no fresh backup, drafts to process…).
adminGrandCentralGuide.s1Li3Rich: <red>Red</red>: a critical service has failed, the vault is in error, or the health check is unresponsive.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s1Li4Rich: Below: shortcuts to <halls>each main hall</halls> (security, compliance, archive, forge, support, code, etc.).
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li1Rich: <m>Security & Perfection</m> — Controls, log, emergency stop, vault; nothing modifies Git alone from there.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li2Rich: <m>Compliance Agent</m> — Index + drafts; the compliance page for the queue; legal publication with human review.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li3Rich: <m>Audit Kit</m> — Clean folder for external audit (evidence, exports).
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li4Rich: <m>Black Box</m> — Cloud attic + search; tidy or search when there is enough history.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li5Rich: <m>AI Forge</m> — Curves, 'yes/no like this' on robot texts, major tidy-up possible on Sundays.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li6Rich: <m>Nexus Live</m> — Support: emergencies at the top, you validate the real actions, brief verdict at the end.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li7Rich: <m>Code Guardian</m> — List of tasks on the code, export for the team; you fix on your branch.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li8Rich: <m>Sentinel</m> — Details of indicators and incidents; leads for repair.
adminGrandCentralGuide.s2Li9Rich: <m>Clients & Legal Publication</m> — Client files here; legal texts + emails on the effective date.
adminGrandCentralGuide.footerRich: The <hint>simple explanations</hint> are in each module card (accordion); the small '?' on titles reminds you of the essentials.

adminInvestorArchiveCompare.introRich: Each PDF is a snapshot of the metrics at the time of the click. The table ranks the exports by ascending date and shows the delta compared to the previous export. The boxes summarise the first → last export of the series.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.emptyPositive: Nothing clear in the series (or stability).
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.emptyNegative: No red signal aggregated first → last.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.emptyWatch: Nothing to report on the technical data.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.spanOne: {count} exports · ~1 day between the first and last
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.spanMany: {count} exports · ~{days} days between the first and last
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletWatchSingle: One export: inter-export deltas will appear from the next.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletWatchEmpty: No archived exports — the comparison will fill after downloads and archiving.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletMrrDown: MRR: decrease {amount}{pctPart} between the first and last archived export.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletCashUp: Stripe treasury (total): +{amount} on the series of exports.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletCashDown: Stripe treasury (total): {amount} on the series of exports.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletSubsUp: Active + trial subscriptions counted: +{n} over the archived period.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletSubsDown: Active + trial subscriptions: {n} over the archived period.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletMarginDown: Margin after variable costs: {pts} vs first export — monitor variable costs or MRR.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletBurnUp: Monthly burn ops: +{amount} — to relate to OpenAI usage / Resend budget.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletBurnDown: Monthly burn ops: −{amount} vs first archived export.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletOpenaiError: Last export: reading OpenAI costs in error ({error}) — partial burn comparison.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.bulletUnmatched: Last export: {count} subscription(s) Stripe without linked profile — quality of matching to monitor.
adminInvestorArchiveCompare.footnote: * Margin % = margin after indicative variable costs (OpenAI + Resend pro-rata MRR), as archived in the extract — not audited. PDFs remain downloadable line by line above.
